RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: D Kelt, M J Greatbatch Et Al
Spotlight: Started $21 when safely held in Open company in sloppy conditions here. A distance specialist. Key contender.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: P Trainor, M J Burns Et Al
Spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Racing well for new stable. Narrow miss in the Lord Mayor's Cup this venue 30 May. Can lead or take a sit.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Bennett Racing P/L, Gaire P/L Et Al
Spotlight: Third outing for new trainer/ this region. Ridden back and never a factor this venue second-up. Should be near peak and can find a few lengths here. Should appreciate stepping up in distance.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: A J A Bosma, Dr K L Samson Et Al
Spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Prominent but got the staggers and surrendered quickly here over a longer assignment. Coming back in distance. Looking elsewhere.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Wylie Dalziel Roy Higgins Racing, Rosemont Stud P/L Et Al
Spotlight: Last win was 27 Oct 2024. Showed a fine turn of foot from the back this track in heavy ground. A few runs under his belt this campaign and one for multiples.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: G P Carmody, World Series Group Et Al
Spotlight: Run down in the shadows after a giving a kick from on pace in the Queen Elizabeth II Cup at Rosehill at this distance. Brings pace and stamina. Has some claims.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: On a quick back-up. Sent out $6 when never got into the race at Ipswich. Has his first look at 2400m here. Very fit and can play a role.

RaceName: Tattersall's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Blueblood Thoroughbreds, B B T Macheera Et Al
Spotlight: Warmed up nicely late at Rosehill at this trip after speccing from $101 into $71. First time around Eagle Farm. Lacks appeal here.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ronball, C McGregor & Miss Z J McGregor
Spotlight: Just wacked away without threatening over 1400m Stradbroke Hcp here at this distance. Proven record wet/track. Can challenge.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: First Light Racing Pty Ltd, G J Walker Et Al
Spotlight: Won the BRC Sprint at Doomben two back 23 May. Sent out $61 when never got into the race in the slosh this venue over this distance. Well into preparation now and one for multiples.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Courageous effort in the Moreton Cup here at resumption. Fitter, proven second-up. At optimum distance. Right in this.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Snowden Racing Pty Ltd, Pinecliff Racing Et Al
Spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Off midfield when flew home to score at Scone at this distance. Since won a Rosehill trial 5 Jun. One for exotics.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: D J McKenzie & Mrs M E McKenzie
Spotlight: On pace and stuck on grittily for narrow Hinkler Hcp win this venue. Trained on track but awkwardly drawn. Shown can handle the wet here. Could add some value to exotics.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Strawberry Hill Stud, D A Norris Et Al
Spotlight: Fantastic effort when picked up smartly and only beaten a whisker here at first run for new trainer. Will benefit from that run. Could get into the placings.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs C L Morton, McHams Et Al
Spotlight: Settled midfield, ran on fairly without threatening this venue. Very fit and can fill the minors here.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Miss S M Parkinson, Mrs K Lovett Et Al
Spotlight: Made late rally from the back at Randwick. Hard to make a case.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Backing up quickly. Put in some good work late at Ipswich at first run for new trainer. First time around Eagle Farm. Others carry more punch.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Last win was 22 Feb 2025. Well back and never a factor in the slosh this track. Usually gets back. Leaning towards others here.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Widden Stud Pty Ltd, Qatar Bloodstock Ltd et al
Spotlight: Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge this track. Fitter second-up. Form solid this distance. Could give some cheek at odds.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: C J Cooke, J B Cooke Et Al
Spotlight: Latest conquest Helen Coughlan Stakes at Eagle Farm 30 May. Really good run in heavy ground coming from the back in Dane Ripper Stakes this track. Looks up against it.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs D O'Toole, G F O'Toole & J O'Toole
Spotlight: Won the 3yo+ Magic Millions F&M Gold Coast 17 Jan. Didn't quite finish it off the last bit in Open company here. One for exotics.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Against own sex when brave effort in busy finish this track. Drops to shorter trip. Can fill the minors here.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers this venue when second-up. Has a tricky draw. Lacks appeal here.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Great run at Doomben 23 May. Snagged back and worked home okay in Dane Ripper Stakes this track. Usually gets back. Could add some value to exotics.

RaceName: Tattersall's Tiara (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Darby Racing, A J Barsby Et Al
Spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Closed solidly but not quick enough here 13 Jun. Switching to 1400m. Others more persuasive.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Yarraman Park Stud, Grant Bloodstock Pty Ltd Syndicate Et Al
Spotlight: Raced in back half and never likely here second-up. Has an adverse draw and up in weight. Needs to turn it around quickly.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs L J Sears, Ms M L Sears & Mrs S E Wee
Spotlight: The Archer winner Rockhampton 5 May. Settled back and didn't figure in heavy ground in the Stradbroke Hcp this track. At optimum distance. Warrants attention.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Wessel Thoroughbreds, M A McLean Et Al
Spotlight: Grand campaigner. Led, gave away final stages here at this distance. Leads, handy. Right in this.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs C L Munce, T D Chivers Et Al
Spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here at this trip. Could get into the placings.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Lees Racing Pty Ltd, P B Jones Et Al
Spotlight: Last win was 28 Feb 2025. Just missed at Scone 16 May. Showed a fine turn of foot from the back at Randwick. First time around Eagle Farm.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Lindsay Park Bloodstock, R & C Legh Racing(R Legh) Et Al
Spotlight: Put in some big strides late this venue over this trip as a market drifter. Usually gets back. Capable of making an impact.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Bronsan Racing Syndicate, C Sands & Est T Marnane
Spotlight: Ranged up but punctured late here. Among the dangers.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ace Bloodstock Racing Pty Ltd, Ace Club Et Al
Spotlight: In close proximity when battled home in heavy ground here. Draw makes the job harder. At optimum distance. One for exotics.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs C E Chapman, A R B Chapman Et Al
Spotlight: Kept finding the line without threatening in the slosh this track at this distance. Not fancied.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Australian Bloodstock, F J Biviano Et Al
Spotlight: Okay at latest when settled back. Others with more appeal.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Monarch Bloodstock, D Micallef Et Al
Spotlight: Ran on with some conviction from back half this venue second-up. Stripping fitter and looks a distant place hope only.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Last assignment this trip when handy and boxed on solidly this venue. One for exotics.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Strong Bloodstock, B Armstrong Et Al
Spotlight: Freshened. Finished well held at Doomben. Awkwardly drawn. Others with stronger claims.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: Winkurra Colts, T D Davis Et Al
Spotlight: Resuming (18-weeks). Two pleasing trial hitouts leading into return and is a first-up winner. Has an adverse draw for return. Has some claims fresh in this line-up.

RaceName: Eureka Stud W.J. Healy Stakes (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)
OwnerName: D Azzopardi, A Bryant Et Al
Spotlight: Raced midfield, let down powerfully here at this distance. Hard fit and one for exotics.

Prix des Lions - Trophee Studio Harcourt (Conditions) (All Weather)

Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Looks the one to beat on what he's done so far, following his debut second with two wins, most recently a 5f Listed event at Chantilly in comfortable style (Aralia and Yuquot behind); pedigree suggests another furlong won't be an issue, so should be involved in the finish.

RaceName: Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Runner-up both starts under this rider, including when behind the debuting Hinta Grey last time at Chantilly over 6f on very soft, looking really green late on while in front; no surprise blinkers go on, so may find some improvement for that headgear.

RaceName: Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ecurie Elag, RPG Bloodstock et al
Spotlight: Made a successful debut over 5.5f at Chantilly last month on good ground in a newcomers maiden (sent off 11-1), with that form relatively untested since; the betting will likely be the best guide to what's expected in this.

RaceName: Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Nicely bred filly who won a 6f conditions race from a wayward Scapin on debut at Chantilly (very soft) having gone off a 9-1 chance; needs to cope with this much faster ground but has lots of potential for better.

RaceName: Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
OwnerName: Thierry Storme, Annua Racing SL et al
Spotlight: Only ever run over 5f at Chantilly; 5l behind Tokaido on her debut before winning a conditions event by 3l; followed that with a 1.5l defeat by Tokaido again in Listed company, so a little bit of work to do with that rival; having her first go over 6f.

RaceName: Prix du Bois - B.P.E Lecieux (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Supplemented for this; won over 5.5f at Nantes on very soft on debut before finishing sixth behind both Tokaido and Aralia in a 5f good ground Listed event at Chantilly last month as a 16-1 chance; needs new trip to bring about improvement; retained jockey Maxime Guyon on for first time.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Bettered underwhelming stable debut when very close second at Happy Valley this month and remains well handicapped on some of his Class 4 form from earlier in the season; leading contender.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Made the frame on consecutive appearances at Happy Valley in April/May (6f/5f) and didn't run badly in a Class 4 there this month; swaps usual blinkers for first-time cheekpieces; has a big role to play from stall 1.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Gained his only win in 2023 but has finished second on three of his five starts in this basement grade, the latest a fortnight ago, and has strong each-way claims again.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed on dirt here last month and wasn't beaten far when seventh from stall 10 at Happy Valley 24 days ago; drawn even wider here but Zac Purton takes the ride; could have a say if he gets a good pace to aim at.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Appreciated ease in grade and ran well from the front when fifth at Happy Valley last month; well drawn to attack here and has good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen enlisted; can make a bold bid.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; managed only a never-dangerous seventh over C&D when dropped to this grade a fortnight ago and has another wide draw.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheng Chung Wah & Enzo Cheng Tin Hang
Spotlight: 14-race maiden; posted sound effort when third over C&D on turf return a fortnight ago, but is drawn much wider today; swaps hood for first-time blinkers; others preferred.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed for first time when keeping-on third over C&D in March, but two down-the-field finishes have followed (7f/6f) and he's now 0-11.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-12 but looked suited by drop back to 6f when fourth at Happy Valley last month and is another 2lb lower here; considered each-way.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Found things happening too quickly over 5f last month and now returns to a more suitable trip, but this regressive 6yo is having a disappointing season and is poorly drawn today.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed twice over 5f at Happy Valley in December/January, but has been out of form lately and his torrid run of luck draw-wise has continued.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Well beaten over 7f here three weeks ago and has now been unplaced in all 15 Hong Kong starts.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: George Lee Hing Fai, Wong Che Leung Et Al
Spotlight: Gave good account when close third at Happy Valley this month and remains on a workable mark, but has modest 1-35 strike-rate; others appeal more.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 18-race maiden; made the frame on three of his five starts this year (5f-6f) and didn't run badly when sixth at Happy Valley (5f) last month, but has become somewhat frustrating.

Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won C&D Class 4 contest in October and was placed at that level last month; has the services of Zac Purton for this first dip into Class 5 and is respected, despite his wide draw.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: No longer the force of old but cashed in on a reduced mark over C&D a fortnight ago and ought to remain competitive after recent 5lb rise.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: 22/23 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Needed considerable help from the local handicapper before opening Hong Kong account at Happy Valley last month, his 16th attempt; 5lb higher now but could plausibly use that success as a springboard.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Michael Chan Se Ping
Spotlight: Just held on for Class 4 C&D win in early part of this season, but has lost his way this year and managed only ninth over C&D when dropped to this grade six weeks ago.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unplaced all nine starts but was a very close fourth (albeit from favourable draw) on recent stable debut at Happy Valley; player if able to build on that performance.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unplaced all 11 starts; suggested he might be building up to something when fifth twice here in March but two down-the-field runs followed and he's poorly drawn today.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Just 1-34 in Hong Kong but largely consistent over 6f-1m for new stable this season and has gradually edged down the weights; probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-19 in Hong Kong but made the frame twice over C&D last month and wasn't beaten far when eighth at Happy Valley 24 days ago; recent 2lb drop helps; can make his presence felt.

OwnerName: David Tse Sik Hung, Allison Tse Pui Shan & Q Tse Pui Ching
Spotlight: Made quite encouraging stable debut when fifth at Happy Valley in April, but last month's C&D run was a backward step and he's now 0-17.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-22 in Hong Kong; started off for current stable with two respectable runs last month but was soundly beaten over C&D a fortnight ago and needs to be perked up by a change of headgear.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed behind Basic Instinct at Happy Valley two starts ago, but this 19-race maiden has a rather mixed record this year and is not the percentage call from stall 11.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Still 3lb higher than for win at Happy Valley in March, but was beaten under 1l when third to Country Dancer over C&D this month and can be dangerous from stall 1.

RaceName: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho
Spotlight: 22-race maiden; commendable second to Basic Instinct from stall 11 at Happy Valley last month, but drawn even wider here and far too inconsistent to rely upon.

OwnerName: Peter Lee Kwok Wah, Tony Lau Hon Kwan & Kenny Cheng Tsin Ki
Spotlight: Won when based in Australia but has poor Hong Kong record and usually runs over 1m+.

Spotlight: Placed three times (once over C&D) in Class 3 this year and went close when dropped to this level in April (6f, dirt); can win in this grade soon but has been dealt a duff hand draw-wise.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed over 7f in early part of this season, but has become disappointing and was well beaten over C&D when dropped to this grade six weeks ago; has the inside stall again but needs a revival.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-17 in Hong Kong, having been campaigned mainly over 7f, but was respectable fifth when dropped to this grade a fortnight ago and, given his tendency to race freely, this drop to 6f is worth a go.

OwnerName: Jimmy Tang Kwok Leung, Ho Kai Man & Ricky Lee Wing Ngai
Spotlight: Managed only ninth from a favourable draw when dropped to this grade at Happy Valley this month and has now been unplaced all five Hong Kong starts; opposable from stall 11.

Spotlight: Lurks on a dangerous mark (even before Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim is considered), but is yet to win this season and disappointed when well drawn at Happy Valley last month; needs to up his game to overcome stall 12 here.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Frankie Pang Ka Yeung & A Pang Ka Wai
Spotlight: Consistent since win off 5lb lower at Happy Valley in December and made the frame over C&D on last two appearances; may not have the early zip to make full use of his favourable draw but ought to be on the premises again.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account with narrow 6f dirt win last month, but safely held off today's mark twice since (turf/dirt); needs to kick on again.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Out of a dual 6f Group 3 winner in Australia; came second in a recent 5f trial and evidently has ability; makes official debut.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Dr John Shum Ding Ping & Dr Lawrence Chan Pui Wing
Spotlight: Struggled to get competitive when a big-priced outsider for recent debut (5f).

Spotlight: Held on well to make all from stall 11 over C&D last month, opening his account at the tenth attempt, and did well to finish fifth off new mark three weeks ago, given he was slowly away and made ground on the outside of the field on the home turn; remains of strong interest.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Still a maiden after six starts but, from stall 12, made smooth headway before going very close over C&D last month; Zac Purton keeps the ride; seems likely to strike soon.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Forced a dead-heat over 1m when dipping into Class 5 in March but soundly beaten back in this higher grade three times since, most recently when dropped back to 6f.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden; outran 105-1 odds when placed at Happy Valley in February, but well down the field there on both subsequent outings and has tough draw.

RaceName: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after six runs (5f-7f) and almost certainly needs a drop in grade.

Spotlight: Patently not the force of old but, having tumbled down the weights, looked unlucky not to finish closer than fifth over C&D on Sunday; recent 2lb drop enables this dip into Class 4 and good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen is now enlisted; must be considered.

RaceName: Nam Chung River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk & Ma Yuen Yu
Spotlight: Kept on well to fend off Enthralled over C&D last month and has now won three times this season; looks vulnerable after latest 6lb rise, though.

RaceName: Nam Chung River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Pipped Volcanic Spark over C&D in March but has suffered a dip in form since and remains on a tough mark.

RaceName: Nam Chung River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not at best this season but handicapped accordingly now and has been placed twice here (1m2f/1m1f) in recent weeks; ought to be right in the thick of things again.

OwnerName: Howard Tai Wai Yin & Yim Hin Keung
Spotlight: Made low-key stable debut over 1m here this month, but that was his first run for nearly a year and he could still have untapped potential over this longer trip; has possibilities if that recent outing brought him on.

RaceName: Nam Chung River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: In-form 6yo who led close home to score over 1m1f here lasrt month and was denied a clear run when fifth over C&D four weeks later; holds each-way claims at least.

Spotlight: Three wins over 6f on turf in Hong Kong including one here; has run even better than it looked in two starts over C&D since dropped into Class 3, drawn wide on the first occasion and meeting trouble in the second; big run likely.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won over C&D in January and should be happier back here after two lesser efforts at Happy Valley; one for the shortlist.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Four wins over 6f on turf including one here; 6lb below his last winning mark, but his form in the meantime has been mixed; others are more compelling.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-4 when in Australia, but just a midfield finish on his local debut at Happy Valley (5f) last month; improvement needed.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Made a winning local debut here (5f) in January, but his form has regressed since; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has made a good start to his career with two wins from three starts over C&D; an 11lb rise asks a stiff question, but he won a barrier trial impressively since last seen so he remains a big player.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ian Chan Yau Nam & Cyrus Chan Sing Kit
Spotlight: Got off the mark in Hong Kong over C&D last month, but disappointing over 7f next time even allowing for the longer trip; opposable from stall 11.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-31 with all bar one of his starts coming here; dual C&D winner and placed on numerous occasions, but not at his best lately.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 1-15, the win coming in a Class 4 over C&D in April; runner-up in this grade next time, but last two efforts have been below par; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-3 in Australia, but has only beaten a couple in three starts over 5f/6f in Hong Kong; drawn widest and best watched for now.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won two Class 4 events over C&D during the winter, but has struggled at Class 3 level since; opposed from his high draw.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 1-2 in Australia, but little in four starts over 5f-7f here; may need a drop in grade before he clicks back into gear.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in two starts over 6f here and at Happy Valley; best watched for now.

RaceName: Shek Sheung River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: All seven wins have come over this trip at Happy Valley, but is 0-17 here; may be eligible for a return to Class 4 company after this.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Has run respectably in four maidens with best effort a second to a highly-regarded Ballydoyle colt over C&D; well beaten in the 6f Coventry Stakes since but can do better back to this trip.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Second foal; dam French 1m2f/10.5f winner (inc Listed; RPR 109), half-sister to useful 1m3f winner Deremah, out of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to French Derby winner Darsi; nicely bred but will appreciate further in time and likely to improve from this.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Well-bred sort was made 4-9 favourite on debut over C&D but couldn't get to grips with a stablemate and Bull Shark; however, will surely be sharper with that run behind him and should take the beating today with Moore staying aboard.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to 1m/1m1f winner Hawk Mountain (inc 2yo Futurity Group 1; RPR 119), half-brother to winners Wingspan (1m/9.4f inc Listed; 114) and Officer (7f 2yo/1m Listed; 112); dam 7f-1m4f winner (inc 2yo/dual Group 1; 119); superbly-bred colt looks the yard second-string but still respected.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc AW/Group 1; RPR 120), half-sister to winners Speak In Colours (6f Group 2) and Pretty In Grey (useful 7f/1m); by a legendary sire and made 1,300,000gns as a yearling; seems yard third-string on debut but likely can run with promise.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 10.6f AW winner Powerful Hook Head (RPR 84); dam German 6.5f 2yo winner (87), half-sister to winners Beauty Crescent (6f 2yo Listed) and Worship (useful 6f 2yo); nicely-bred sort has the credentials to run well on debut, so check the market.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest when tackling handicaps.

RaceName: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to 1m4f AW (RPR 80) and 2m1f hurdle winner Cinnamon Coco; dam unraced sister to 7f Group 3 winner Talaayeb; by a leading sire and cost E140,000 as a yearling; seems yard's first-string on jockey bookings and can run well.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 1-5 in France then 0-7 in Ireland; midfield in York handicap (1m2f) on stable/seasonal debut when the 150-1 rank outsider; perhaps best watched unless the market signals go the other way.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Has won over 1m but raced mainly at 7f; not solid on 2026 form and he came up short when a 50-1 shot in big field at York most recently; opposed in another competitive race, back up in trip.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 2-3 on Kempton AW; 0-8 on turf but has been placed in half of those runs, most recently at Musselburgh on seasonal debut; could go well with Oisin Murphy up for first time.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Ran encouragingly in first couple of starts for new stable but has posted two duck eggs kept to 7f since, latest in competitive field at York; doesn't look as if he's about to add to his Irish wins.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Strike-rate is 6-26; soon in a consistent vein of form this term and was beaten only half a length by the market leader in York event (1m1f, good) most recently, taking record at this venue to 512; solid.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Rather quirky sort who has become a slow-starter and is 0-12 since a successful debut at the 2024 York Dante meeting but is threatening to double his tally; met traffic issues on his way to a very notable effort at this venue (7f, good) most recently, beating all bar a handicap snip who remains unbeaten and looks a Group performer in the making; shaped as if this second attempt over 1m is well worth exploring; highly respected.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Record at Redcar comprises his two wins for previous yard and best effort for new stable; York form comprises poor efforts in this race last year and in latest outing; something to prove.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Raced exclusively at shorter, gaining most wins over 6f; looks likely to be outstayed and outclassed upped in trip/grade.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and hasn't prevailed in this sphere since York success three years ago; midfield when the 125-1 rank outsider back here last time.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Two from two as a 2yo, both starts on Kempton AW; has finished last in a couple of turf handicaps this season but a subsequent gelding operation may prompt a turnaround and he represents a major trainer whose last two York runners, both in competitive races two Saturdays ago, were successful; by no means written off.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 4-15 overall and 2-5 since wearing visor; campaigned exclusively over shorter but the manner of his half-length success in big field at York (7f, good) last time suggests this new trip is worth exploring; enters calculations in the retained headgear.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Has failed to convince over 1m2f since returning to home soil; gelded prior to latest start; 1m winner during his campaign at Meydan and the return to this distance looks the angle with him.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Scored three times last term for previous yard; below par in latest outing but ran well at Haydock (1m) earlier in May; this 3yo looks a possible danger off a favourable weight, provided he bounces back.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap
Spotlight: In-form 3yo who is 2-6 since handicapping; fitted with cheekpieces in last two starts, winning at Hamilton then creditable fifth in notable event at Carlisle, both over 1m; likely player off bottom weight in the retained headgear.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Had the best draw when third in a C&D novice on debut (good; 11-1); didn't run as well when last of four on softer ground at Carlisle next time; a possible but not ideally drawn.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Gelding by Cotai Glory; fair fourth in a Southwell novice on debut (5f, 13-8 favourite); has since been gelded and is should improve as a result.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in two 6f contests at York (good), last time finishing 2l behind an odds-on Godolphin newcomer; plum draw; sets the standard.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Similar level of form when fourth in a Newmarket novice (5f, good to firm; 15-8 favourite) and in a conditions event at Beverley (5f, good to firm); may improve for the step up to 6f.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 82,000gns at the breeze-up; well beaten fifth of six in a Haydock novice on last month's debut (6f, soft 13-2); needs to improve on this faster ground.

RaceName: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Filly by Havana Grey who has been well beaten in two C&D novice events (good, good to soft); others stronger.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: 1-1 on AW, namely a 7f novice a Kempton last March; recorded a big personal best when winning the Group 2 Park Stakes at Doncaster (7f, soft) and his two defeats since have been excusable; tough task trying to give weight away back sprinting after 133 days off.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: 7f novice win here in November but last of 22 on handicap debut at York (7f, good) five weeks ago; stones to find in this company.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Made all to win the corresponding race 12 months ago; 2-2 on AW this year and he has performed with credit in Group 3/2 company on turf on his last two starts; should be in the thick of it again.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Third to Diligent Harry in this race 12 months ago; four creditable runs on AW earlier in the year but others bring more pressing claims.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Wouldn't judge him harshly on his two turf defeats this summer but even his best AW form needs bettering if he is to come out on top here.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: 7f AW win as a 2yo; enjoyed his finest hour when winning the Jersey Stakes at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm) 12 months ago; only seen twice since, pulling up at Goodwood last July and then finishing a remote last of five in a French Group 3 (soft ground) last month; not solid but he'll be a danger to all if back near that Jersey Stakes form.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Ended 2024 with a runaway win in a 7f handicap at Southwell; created another big impression when winning a conditions event at Thirsk (7f, good) on last season's reappearance and he was a good third of eight upped to Group 3 company when last seen a year ago; drops to 6f for the first time but this strong traveller should have the pace to cope; still has potential.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Won a Group 2 in Ireland in July 2024; not added to that success since but he has run well in two of his three starts this time round, latterly finishing third of eight in a Group 3 at Epsom (7f, good to soft); drops in trip for his AW debut.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Useful on turf, recording peak RPR when landing a Group 2 at Haydock (6f, good) last May; not at his best in two runs in Dubai this winter; switch to AW shouldn't be an issue and he has the ability to feature.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Had wind surgery prior to an impressive performance over C&D on Good Friday; struggled in a Newbury handicap six weeks ago; stable sent out the winner of this race in 2022, 2023 and 2024; work to do at this level.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Ran poorly on his reappearance at Salisbury (6f, good) five weeks ago; best efforts give him a squeak and he is effective on AW but others appeal more for win purposes.

RaceName: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Well-bred colt cost Gns1.7m as a yearling; just denied on debut over C&D on soft before winning with plenty to spare over 6f at Naas; cracking run in the Coventry Stakes behind a stablemate, not beaten far, and a repeat of that will make him difficult to beat.

RaceName: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
OwnerName: Eric Griffiths, Mrs T Burns & Partner
Spotlight: Maiden winner at Bath ran respectably in a Sandown Listed event next time; below form in softer ground in the Woodcote at Epsom; this surface will suit better, but needs to find a new level of form.

RaceName: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
OwnerName: Ramiro Restrepo & Jacob West & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Got off the mark on debut in a 6f maiden here before a fine effort in the Group 3 Marble Hill Stakes; was 2.5l behind the Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef then, so doesn't have much ground to make up on Confucius on collateral form.

RaceName: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: A 260,000GBP breeze-up buy; got up late to win a Goodwood novice over 6f (soft) on debut; beaten 9l in the Coventry Stakes, but is better than that as he didn't have much racing room late on; can get closer to Confucius today.

RaceName: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Ran creditably in decent maidens at Naas and Navan before getting off the mark at Cork over 6f; that form hasn't been tested since, but it is likely he will have to find significant improvement; seems yard's second-string.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-3 in New Zealand but failed to beat a rival on his local debut over C&D last month; had stall 14 then, but still has enough to prove.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Record over C&D this year reads 21141; up another 7lb for latest win and not so well drawn this time, but still hard to dismiss in his current mood.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Charlie Lam Leung Seng & Michael Lam Yau Keng
Spotlight: Three wins over C&D, but none since September 2024 and the drop to this grade hasn't made much difference; needs a resurgence.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Three wins over 7f/1m here since November; has had a double-figure draw in his last three starts (placed in two), but is drawn against the rail this time; respected.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun
Spotlight: All three local wins over 6f at Happy Valley, the latest in February; didn't enjoy much luck when behind a couple of these over C&D last time, but still to prove he really wants this far.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheung Kwok Wing & Cheung Kwok Keung
Spotlight: Has done well in his four starts, finishing a length behind Aeroinvincible when third over C&D last month; still unexposed and a 7lb pull gives him a good chance of reversing the placings.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ultra-consistent; running well over 1m at Happy Valley lately, but both local wins have come over C&D; no reason why he shouldn't run his race again.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Dual winner in Britain, but hasn't yet taken off in Hong Kong; best watched despite another 2lb drop.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-4 in Australia, but well held in two starts over 6f/7f here; best watched until showing a bit more spark.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: All four wins have come over 6f including one here; twice runner-up in six starts over C&D, but drawn wider than ideal.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Four wins in New Zealand, but not threatened in five starts in Hong Kong and is drawn widest; others preferred.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Yeung Chun Wang, Yeung Ho Wang & Yeung Chung Wang
Spotlight: Has run some good races since winning over C&D in November, but he has a bit to do from stall 13.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Contrasting efforts in two starts here, finishing fourth over 6f on the first occasion but not so good over 7f in the second; his win in Australia came over this trip; too soon to write off.

RaceName: Tan Shan River Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but well held in three starts over 6f in Hong Kong; cheekpieces given a go but he may need a drop in class.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Looked above-average in commencing his career with an emphatic win at Doncaster and creditable fourth in C&D Listed event, both this spring; didn't fare so well at Sandown last time but may still have progress in him; bids to defy top weight on handicap debut.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Good second in sales and Listed races over 6f at York during his 2yo campaign; not in anything like the same form this term, including here, and still needs to prove that he's trained on; record is only 1-12.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Has a largely solid record and may remain competitive despite taking an 8lb hike for an emphatic success, having bossed a small field, at Redcar (5f, good) last Saturday; good third of 13 over C&D the time before and doesn't necessarily need to dominate.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Has very encouraging form switched to handicaps this term, namely a solid second (split Fortification and Naana's Shadow) over C&D then respectable 12th of 27 when pitched into deeper waters at Royal Ascot; may still have further improvement in her; enters calculations.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Has maintained his consistency by posting form figures of 143, all in sprints at York, since joining current stable; beat Stargazed by half a length on the first occasion and ran well, despite a tricky draw the way things panned out, in notable handicap most recently; very solid claims.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Record is only 1-10 but she's fairly useful and has performed well in 5f contests (handicap/Listed) at Sandown this term; perhaps best not to take latest effort literally but in going back up just 1lb the assessor hasn't overreacted; again has frame possibilities.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Has gained both wins in Scotland, including at Musselburgh (5f, good; did it readily) latest start; generally consistent sort but will need further progress to defy a 5lb rise and follow up in this field.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Improving filly who justified favouritism under Oisin Murphy in the 3yo Dash at Epsom most recently, taking overall strike-rate to 3-8 and record in handicaps to 2-4; good third behind Fortification and Stargazed over C&D the time before; strong contender.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Irish sprinter who is building up a solid record; successful at Cork (soft) and good third at the Curragh (good to yielding) since dropped from 6f to 5f; may be capable of better still, particularly over this distance; firmly in the mix.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f but gives the impression this return to 5f may prove beneficial and thus looks an interesting contender off a mark 7lb lower than his peak one; proved resurgent behind a stronger finisher at Kempton (AW) last time.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Scored over C&D last October but is reasonably exposed now and faces a stiffer assignment in this field; came up well short in a notable 6f handicap back here last time.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Not disgraced in the York/Epsom races won by Fortification and Naana's Shadow but is fairly exposed at this point, making her a similar type to stablemate Go Vince Go.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: Held on gamely at Beverley to add to her AW wins; respectable sixth to Naana's Shadow at Epsom since, albeit without showing any further progress; has perhaps reached her ceiling.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap
Spotlight: In-form filly who prevailed by a neck in Class 4 contest over C&D (good) last time; however, needs further improvement to defy her new mark and follow up in this significantly better race.

RaceName: Absolut Tabasco Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Confirmed the promise of his debut third at Catterick last October (7f, good; trained by Ed Walker) when getting off the mark in a Thirsk maiden earlier this month (7f, good to soft; beat a subsequent handicap winner by 5l); stiffer task here, but should go well.

RaceName: Absolut Tabasco Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well-related; beaten 22l in a Carlisle novice on last month's debut (7f, soft; 33-1); should improve, particularly on this better ground.

RaceName: Absolut Tabasco Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Placed on all five starts (first two on the AW) last time getting caught on the line when short-head second at Leicester (1m, good; made the running); drops in trip (has been third over 7f, but also tried at 1m2f); contender.

RaceName: Absolut Tabasco Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in a valuable Haydock novice on second start last September (6f, good); didn't progress on the AW and was well beaten 11th of 12 in a decent Newcastle handicap last time (6f); return to 7f should suit.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Not in peak form (hurdles or Flat) from last August onwards but it's not forgotten that he was third in the Plate itself last year off 3lb higher.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: On his third trainer and has rather chequered form but well handicapped judged on best efforts, including his fourth at Southwell (2m, AW) in December off 7lb higher; first and second over hurdles for new yard this spring, with a never-dangerous ninth in the Chester Cup in between; worth a second look.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Held up; a superb 2024 included wins in the Chester Plate, this race and the Cesarewitch at Newmarket; 2025 campaign was initially promising then frustrating, including with trouble in running, but ended up being disappointing; he's back to his last winning mark, but that's having been always behind at 40-1 in the Chester Cup last time; he will surely spark again at some point but it's hard to know exactly when.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Pretty consistent in seven AW runs and well backed when leading final 1f to win the 17-runner Chester Plate (2m2f) on last month's return to turf; up 5lb but he's a rising force.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Industrious and usually dependable sort at 1m6f-2m2f; turf strike-rate is 1-20 and he was tailed off at Thirsk last time; back to AW and he's 7-34 in this sphere, but he's not shone at this meeting (16th in the Plate, 12th in the Vase) in the last two years.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Very busy and he's had his share of wins, including four (one here) over 2m in 2025; AW has dominated his career; three of his last four turf starts were respectable and he's edging down the weights, so not entirely dismissed.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: 10yo, no significant impact when very lightly raced in recent years; his only two AW races (neck second in one) were on Polytrack in 2018 and 2019.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Very solid record in 2024 on turf but 592 days off before this season; beaten a long way on his return (his only AW race) and not so far next time but he still has major work to do to turn things around.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Returned to Flat with short-priced wins (2m, good) at Ascot and Chester, leading over 2f out; firmly in command last time and looks well treated under a 5lb penalty; well beaten in two maidens here in 2023 but a close second in Polytrack handicap in 2025, and that's the sum of his AW record; hitting new heights on turf and a huge player if he's as effective on this surface.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Stays 1m6f; two Flat wins include a Polytrack handicap at Dundalk in January 2025; went the wrong way last summer/autumn, including over hurdles, and this is his first run since leaving Dermot Weld for £4,000 in April.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France last autumn, from just four races there; picked up for 105,000euros in November and put up a bold show when fourth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) last month; that looked most encouraging but he was tailed off at Goodwood (2m, soft; upset in the stalls) three weeks ago; starkly contrasting messages going into this AW debut.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Front-runner who did well throughout five-race campaign last season, most notably when gamely winning this race; 5lb higher today but much more of a worry is that he needs to bounce back from this season's two poor runs on turf.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Thriving since the cheekpieces went on in December, winning six of his nine starts in them and that includes his last four races (on Southwell Tapeta four runs back); the big majority of those races were on AW, with a comfortable success at Lingfield last time (has never run at this track); up another 5lb and into a better race but he may well carry on the good work.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Usually races prominently; 4-7 for new yard this year, including two wins on Southwell Tapeta (unraced here); fifth to Shrimp Shady in Chester Plate and ran well again when second at Thirsk last time; each-way chance.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Disappointing on penultimate start but in good form over 1m4f otherwise this year, with the first of those runs at this track; tried 2m twice last summer and ran well on first occasion despite racing freely; needs to raise his game again, though.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt in a 2m handicap at Dundalk (2m, Polytrack) in December; this season he's been a never-dangerous eighth to Shrimp Shady at Chester (2m2f) and helped set the pace when sixth of 18 at Fairyhouse (1m6f; just 4-1), not discredited in either but a prominent finish in today's race would be much his best handicap performance.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Off the mark when stepping up in trip at Ripon (2m, good; made all) in April; third in a warm Thirsk race on penultimate start, followed by a tidy win at 8-11 in a four-runner race at Catterick this Monday; that gives him a 5lb penalty today and sole AW run was in a novice (okay) but he has more to offer with stamina tests and needs considering.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: 8yo who has run 19 times at Newcastle (vastly more than any other track) and had two wins here plus five seconds; second in this race in 2022 and 12th in 2024; his most fruitful trip this year has been 1m2f but he won over 1m6f last November and was a C&D second last August; has a competitive mark too, for a stable in good form.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
OwnerName: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Switched to front-running this year, including with Tapeta wins over 1m4f here in March and 2m at Southwell in April when last seen; that's career-best AW form but this race is a much higher grade and should be much harder to boss.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)
Spotlight: Dual French Flat winner on soft in 2024, who also won a Listowel maiden hurdle last June; he's seemingly sparked only once in six starts since and while he brings a solid AW record, none of that's much beyond 1m4f; his trainer (same as Alphonse Le Grande) always warrants a second look, though.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Dual 2yo winner including over 6f at this track; won a Fairyhouse handicap last year over this trip but ran below that level twice after; has to be at her best on return from an absence.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Has won three times on the AW including a 1m Listed race at Dundalk last November; nearly took advantage of a lower turf mark when third on return in a 6f handicap at this track, but was below that form since at Cork; has to rebound.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Has hit form lately, winning two 7f handicaps at Gowran on easy ground, beating Greydreambeliever on both occasions; raised 6lb for latter success and handles faster ground fine, so has to be on the shortlist with claimer staying aboard.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Won for the second time when taking a 1m Leopardstown handicap on return; below that form at Leopardstown and Naas since; found to be lame after last time, so has to bounce back.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
OwnerName: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck
Spotlight: Won on 2yo debut for Karl Burke but failed to win again, albeit some decent runs; solid efforts in defeat all starts for this yard including twice denied by Rion Rubette at Gowran; one for the shortlist.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Won a 2yo maiden on debut at Gowran for Jessica Harrington and some good runs in defeat after without winning; well beaten on debut for this stable over 6f here, but that was on testing ground and can do a lot better now back on faster.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a 6f handicap on soft here in April and followed up on good ground at Naas next time over the same trip; beaten 5l in a competitive 7f Cork handicap since and will have to do better now.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Won three times last year, an AW maiden (1m) plus handicaps at Leopardstown (1m) and Tipperary (7.5f); outclassed in a Group 3 on return and probably found the ground too soft since; can do better on this faster ground.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Won a 5f maiden on soft at Navan last autumn and doubled her tally in a 7f Leopardstown handicap (yld/sft) in April; below form twice since and this ground might be a bit too lively for her.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Has shown improved form since tackling handicaps, winning at Down Royal (7f), here over 6.5f and Leopardstown back over 7f; up another 8lb, but handles this ground and has to be respected.

RaceName: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
Spotlight: Maiden owes her high mark to finishing fourth in the Group 3 Anglesey Stakes at this meeting last year; not a bad effort on her first run since at Fairyhouse last time and should improve from that; 1lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Useful but is 0-12 since completing a Doncaster novice double in spring 2024, essentially proving quite difficult to place; hasn't strictly improved for a switch to hold-up tactics this term (seemingly being taught to settle better); unlikely to regain the winning thread on these terms.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Comes from off the pace; has made the frame several times at Group level since his Listed win 13 months ago; not disgraced at Epsom most recently, especially considering the race panned out unfavourably; however, chance again partly depends on whether the cards drop right.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Smart performer; completed a notable hat-trick (on good to firm ground) last summer, culminating in Group 1 honours over C&D; avoids a penalty for that success and holds a leading chance assuming he's back in similar form; best to forgive Epsom Group 3 effort last time (unsuited by the way the race unfolded).

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Not totally solid on his form since 150-1 success when pacemaker in last year's Sussex Stakes (1m, good); however, looks interesting back down in trip, with his 7f record reading 1211225 (ran creditably behind Never So Brave in C&D Group 1 the final time); fitting of tongue-tie may also contribute to a resurgence.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Always behind in the boiling cauldron of a Royal Ascot handicap last week; progressive otherwise and still has good stats, being 9-22 overall, 2-3 this term and 6-11 over 7f (1-1 over C&D); however, has to overcome a 3lb penalty for the Group 3 win achieved against her own sex in May.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Ran below expectations when a warm order for the Jersey Stakes at Royal Ascot last Saturday but was drawn a bit higher than ideal, the way the race panned out, and finishing sixth of 16 wasn't a total disaster; 2-2 previously, including 7f Listed win at Newmarket, and can be treated as retaining potential; respected in receipt of 9lb from his elders.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Finished strongly and nearly got up from a difficult position in 5f contest at York (good) last month; respectable eighth of 28 when stepped back up to 7f for the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) last week; enters calculations.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Richard Hannon; off the mark for the Quinn team when making virtually all in a 22-runner handicap at York Dante meeting (6f, good) in May and bounced straight back from a lesser run at Thirsk when 2l second of seven at Ripon (6f, good) nine days ago; same mark this afternoon; bold show likely from a handy draw.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Back to form to take advantage of a reduced mark at Hamilton (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; put back up 8lb and has gained all six wins on going softer than good.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Won the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June and left reappearance behind when good fourth of 20 in the Dash there (5f, good to soft) three weeks ago; just as effective at 6f and can play a prominent role from an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Seems best on the AW these days and has run poorly on his last three turf outings; his hopes hinge on a recent wind operation having a positive effect.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month, proving suited by the return to turf and belatedly registering a first win since joining Ian Williams; a 3lb rise still leaves him feasibly handicapped on the best of last year's form.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: 6yo who posted career-best performance when dominating from the front over C&D (good) last month; used up energy to get across to lead from a wide draw when fifth of ten over 5f here a fortnight ago and can be treated as if still in good form.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: A ready winner at Bath (1m, firm) in April (final start for Stuart Williams); good start for Jennie Candlish when third over 1m1f at Ayr (good to firm) but not in the same form when beaten favourite at Carlisle (1m) ten days later; quite a strong traveller over those trips but whether he has the speed for sprinting remains to be seen.

RaceName: Manor Car Hire Handicap
Spotlight: Did well to overcome stall 12 over C&D (good) at the end of May; failed to fire in a valuable 3yo handicap at York (22 runners) a fortnight ago but a lot better can be expected up against her elders in a less competitive race, particularly as she's bagged the inside stall.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Prolific 8yo who gained his 11th success when taking 1m4f Ripon handicap in April; also effective at 2m this term, including C&D, but he's edged back up the weights; place player but he may be vulnerable for win purposes.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Off since a mixed hurdles campaign ended in March; formerly very smart on Flat but he's not scored in this sphere since 2023, had a disappointing 2025 and all wins have been on soft or heavy ground; tailed off in this race in 2024.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Third win last term was clearcut over 1m4f here in August and he ran creditably over 1m6f on turf next time; it's not that hard to forgive this season, given the 1m2f/1m, although he has raced a bit freely; looked the type to make progress this year and this is an eye-catching rethink on trip.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Broke his losing run in clearcut style when he reappeared at Kempton (2m, AW) in March with restored cheekpieces, which have been kept since; good second in valuable race at Newmarket (1m6f) but he needs to bounce back from a disappointing show at Goodwood five weeks ago.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Beat Dancing In Paris by a neck to win this race last year off 6lb lower; also won at Chester in September and two of his three starts this term have been creditable, so he's in the each-way picture.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: J William Rowley & S P C Woods
Spotlight: Progressive on AW last autumn, including here, and another improved effort when he won a five-runner race at Goodwood (upped to 2m, good) this May; mid-division at 14-1 in the 2m4f Ascot Stakes 11 days ago, not discredited but he probably needs to resume improvement in another major handicap today.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: He hasn't won since August 2024 but ran some fine races last season, including when beaten a neck by Spirit Mixer in this event; 4lb higher today and made a muted reappearance at Epsom three weeks ago but that was at 40-1 over 1m4f on soft; should be more interesting again with that under his belt.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: With tongue-tie enlisted he's hit major new heights for new yard this year; started it with wins from off the pace at Newcastle (2m, AW) and Musselburgh (1m6f, soft), both in some style, before his excellent fifth of 15 from out the back in the 2m2f Chester Cup; a bit slow to find his stride that day and cheekpieces now go on; more to give if he takes to them, so a major player.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ended last year in storming form with a 1m6f/2m hat-trick, the final leg on Southwell Tapeta; not disgraced when never dangerous here in January and made a satisfactory return to turf (second at Newbury) in April, but this 8yo needs to hit another new high.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: J Blackburn & Mrs C A Coward
Spotlight: Well beaten in 2018 in his only AW race; peak Flat form came with big-race placings over 2m4f in summer 2021; there's been a four-year absence since and his 2m win at Beverley (2lb higher today) this April showed that stacks of his ability remains, form which was backed up at Thirsk on latest start but this contest demands more; cheekpieces (absent this term) return.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Three wins at this track but he came 13th in this race last year; that was his only appearance over this long a trip and he was also below form when he had a crack at 1m6f, so he's unproven beyond 1m4f, over which he's contested his last nine starts; stamina is the big doubt.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: The Cat And The Mag Partnership
Spotlight: Four wins (three AW) over about 1m2f from 22 races in France; 50-1 fourth at York (1m4f) in May was an encouraging third run for Brian Ellison, after which he was never dangerous at 25-1 off this reduced mark in the 1m2f Zetland Gold Cup at Redcar; tongue tied every time except for those last two starts and he is again today; chief conundrum, though, has to be with this first attempt beyond 1m4f.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Last season included four wins and a good C&D second to Sax Appeal; a hard-working winner at evens in four-runner race at Lingfield (1m6f, good) last month, which he needs to improve on if he's to defy a 5lb penalty but that was a solid start to his 4yo campaign and he could still have potential.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Brought over from Ireland for five British races and ran well every time, including when fourth in this contest last year and third in the Chester Plate last month; he didn't win any of them, though, and has taken just one Irish handicap; plenty seemingly needs to go right if he's to win this but he has a place chance despite wide draw.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won 1m2f maiden last June; on the premises in all his four handicaps, coming from off the pace when second at Haydock (1m6f) on final 3yo start and when third at Hamilton (1m5f; gelded and hooded) on reappearance six weeks ago; relatively lightly raced, so could have further improvement in him and 2m is plausible for this AW debut.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Third turf win was a narrow one off this mark at Newbury (2m, good; rallying) in April on his final start last year; ran creditably once in 2025 on Polytrack and it's not hard to make excuses for his three turf runs this year, including the Chester Cup, but a revival is needed and such a wide draw probably makes that harder.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Three AW wins include his only appearance at this track, over C&D in 2023; sole win since was in March 2024; excellent third off this mark in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket that October but unraced since until well held at 66-1 over 1m4f one month ago; whether that run is enough to have brought him back to peak form is hard to say with confidence but he shaped okay.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two novice hurdles last autumn seem a pretty meagre return for respected yard but he was an unlucky, strong-finishing third in the 2024 Chester Cup; glimpses of that Flat ability on occasions since, but he made inroads far too late from out the back in this last year (11th at 13-2) and it was a similar story in last month's Chester Cup (8th at 11-1); something needs to change and such a wide draw probably means he tries to come from the back again.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has Polytrack form, most of it in his Irish days with Henry De Bromhead; well treated on some 2025 form but he's not shown enough for new yard this season (tailed off over C&D first occasion); cheekpieces are recruited.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 1m win on the first of five runs in France; never dangerous at 50-1 and 25-1 with cheekpieces over 1m4f when he was finally seen in Britain for two handicaps last month but he did make some late headway, particularly when fifth of 13 on Kempton Polytrack last time; marked overall improvement is needed but he's a lightly raced, unexposed 4yo stepping up in trip.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Was beaten 6l on debut by the Queen Mary winner Victorious; improved on that when winning a Listowel maiden over 6.5f, doing well after being pestered throughout; looks the stable second-string on riding arrangements, but could still run well.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
OwnerName: OMEGA Racing & Belle Morris & Russell B Jones Jr
Spotlight: Improved nicely from her debut fifth over C&D to win a 5f Cork maiden on soft ground; is a speedy sort who is possibly better suited to 5f; a hood is added to help her relax; can go well but vulnerable.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Ran a cracker on debut to be beaten only a neck in a Naas Group 3 by the subsequent Queen Mary winner Victorious; was disappointing in the Windsor Castle Stakes 10 days ago but can be a player if resuming her progression.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Did a fair bit wrong on her debut over C&D last month but still showed promise when a 5l second to the re-opposing Sun Goddess; should get closer to that rival today and looks the stable's selection of two here.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
Spotlight: A bargain yearling purchase at only Eur6,500; won a Cork auction maiden on debut over 6f on soft; the runner-up was since second to Cleodolinda at Navan; faster ground today but could go well if handling it.

RaceName: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Looked in need of the experience when just denied on debut at Naas; was impressive when beating Green Empress by 5l over C&D next time; well backed when a close second in the Albany Stakes at Royal Ascot; is the one to beat if turning out fresh after just eight days rest.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Pineapple Stud And Miss C Douglas
Spotlight: Back to best when defeating the reopposing Magic Basma (La Brodeuse fourth, Brazilian Rose fifth) in 7f Musselburgh Listed race (good) on reappearance three weeks ago; more will be needed to defy a penalty but should be able to get a good position from her inside stall.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: This 5yo is in the form of her life at present, gaining a third Musselburgh handicap win of the season when easily seeing off seven rivals 37 days ago; had a wide stall as a an excuse for a slightly below-par effort over the extended 7f here prior to that and she went close here last season; on the shortlist.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Four handicap wins on the Southwell AW this January-March (7f/6f); ran right up to her best when 3.5l fifth of seven to Circe in 7f Musselburgh Listed race latest but it underlines she needs more at this level; a wide draw won't make it any easier.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Hooded when winning Naas Listed event (good) and Leopardstown Group 3 (soft) over 7f for Michael O'Callaghan last year; below form tackling firmer ground when fifth in 7f Lingfield Group 3 on return for Andrew Balding (same owner); good chance if recapturing her best.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Just failed to win a 7f Ascot Listed event (soft; unlucky) last autumn but in October but she needs to put a couple of underwhelming efforts this year behind her.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: 4yo who has shown improved form in Listed races this term, finishing runner-up at Haydock (6f, good to soft) and Musselburgh (7f, good) on last two outings; Circe was 1.25l ahead of her at the latter but she's 3lb better off with Richard Hannon's charge this time; each-way claims again.

RaceName: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Back on form to take advantage of a reduced mark in 7f Musselburgh handicap (good) three weeks ago; this is harder but the fact she was second in a Group 3 in France as a juvenile provides some hope.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Progressive six-time winner whose last two successes came over 2m here (good to soft/good) in July and September; outclassed in a course Group 3 last month but had previously been running respectably and this return to handicaps will see her in much better light; considered.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a Hamilton maiden (1m3f) and handicap (1m5f) on his first two starts of last year; finished tailed off in the Melrose over C&D next time, however, and he weakened quickly before being dismounted at Musselburgh on his comeback; difficult to suggest after that.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: No wins since an odds-on debut success for Karl Burke back in 2023 but he's run with credit on plenty of occasions; that wasn't the case at Chester last time, however, and on balance he's opposable despite this career-low mark.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Handles most surfaces and his eight wins include two here, latterly over 2m in 2024; last scored at Catterick (1m4f) off 3lb higher in October and after some lesser efforts this season he was much more competitive when third back there (1m6f) a fortnight ago; contender to step forward again and he's considered.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won here (2m, good to firm) in July but an 8lb rise signalled a reversal in his fortunes and he's beaten one horse in both starts this term; nothing wrong with this mark if the visor prompts a turnaround, though.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 7f winner at Thirsk (soft) in September and he was back in that sort of form when close third at Hamilton (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; sire and damsire both influences for stamina but this marked step up in trip is still a little surprising.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Progressive 3yo who landed three-runner event on his handicap debut at Hamilton (1m3f) in May and went very close to defying a 4lb rise in another small field at Doncaster one week ago; up another 4lb but there's plenty of stamina in the family and this trip can unlock more.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Built on promising return over 1m2f when seeing out 1m4f strongly to land three-runner novice at Beverley four weeks ago; almost certainly capable of better, particularly over this trip, and he's a very interesting newcomer to handicaps.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: 2024 Gimcrack winner; good third at Lingfield on his belated reappearance before winning at Wolverhampton (7f, Listed; fine ride) in March; below par on turf twice since but likely to make a bold bid back on AW.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Began the year with a pair of impressive 5f handicap wins on AW (here and at Southwell) and he didn't have ideal track position when finishing in midfield in a Naas Listed event in April; too bad to be true at York last time; equally effective at 5f and 6f; remains of interest.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Useful as 2yo sprinter; successful when sights lowered at Yarmouth last September and his third in a 6f Listed race at Doncaster (good to soft) in March was his best form; hard to call him well handicapped now though.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Heavily backed when winning a 6f handicap at Southwell on New Year's Day; ran well over C&D a fortnight later but short of his best twice since, including after wind surgery when eighth of 22 at York last month; returning to AW can help and seems highly likely to run his race.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Smart performer on his day; finished second in the Group 3 on this card 12 months ago and ended 2025 with an Ascot win at that level; it has been a mixed back so far in 2026 but he is edging down the weights and has too much ability to discount; accessories tried last month are now dispensed with.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
