Spotlight: Faced with his optimum conditions but he is 3lb higher than for his win in this race last summer; each-way claims once again.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Three runs at Newcastle, winning two of them and desperately unlucky in the 3yo handicap last Good Friday on the other; looked set for another productive season when scoring at Kempton in March and a subsequent York defeat shouldn't be used as conclusive evidence the handicapper has caught up; leading claims back on AW.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive at 7f in 2025 and he returned from ten months off to run out an easy winner of a Class 3 handicap over C&D (2-2 at Newcastle) in March; up 7lb but foolish to think he's not up to the task; stable won this in 2024.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Reliable operator on AW and after wins at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton he found only Tuco Salamanca too strong in his hat-trick bid in March; better off with that rival today but not sure he's quite so progressive.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
OwnerName: John Murphy & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: Swept through from off the pace to win a good C&D handicap in January; well beaten in two runs since and surprising if there weren't a few stronger in this field.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: The addition of blinkers saw him take a good step forward when comfortably seeing off 17 rivals at Newbury (6f, soft) last October; absent until Derby day three weeks ago where he was never a threat; the blinkers now return and dangerous to underestimate him.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Improved with each of his 3yo starts, getting off the mark in a 5f novice at Southwell last September; ran creditably in Dubai earlier in the year without looking ahead of his mark; returns to action in a tough race and the new visor needs to have a positive effect.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Form took off once handicapping in a tongue tie and his impressive C&D win on Good Friday, his fifth on the bounce, was off 32lb higher than the first; progress ground to a halt on turf last month but not out of the question the return to AW will perk him back up.

RaceName: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap
Spotlight: Twice a C&D winner; ended losing run with a hard-fought success at Leicester in April and he ran creditably at Epsom on Derby day; minor money perhaps his best hope.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Four-time winner on AW; 0-6 on turf but had performed well on her last two starts; 66-1, made much of the running when second of 12 in a 1m Group 2 at this venue last month, with One Look in fourth; raced prominently before fading into fifth (of 15) in the Duke Of Cambridge Stakes at Royal Ascot; suited by quick ground.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Very talented 5yo who raced exclusively over 1m4f in her four races last year; won at Group 2/3 level at Haydock before excellent form in defeat in two Group 1s, second to Minnie Hauk at York and to Kalpana at Ascot; started the new season brightly with odds-on win at Carlisle (1m3f); handled quick ground then, but reckoned to be best on a slower surface.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Course Group 3 winner over 1m last year, but seems most effective at 1m2f, judged on her placed efforts in the Blandford Stakes and the Prix de l'Opéra (just in front of Wemightakedlongway); later, in rear in Ascot race in which Estrange was second; should be better for her run behind shorter-priced stablemate City of Memphis over 1m here last month.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Progressive last term, landing a Listed event at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) and the Group 2 Prix de Malleret at Longchamp (1m4f, good to soft); struggled in the Yorkshire Oaks and Prix de l'Opéra; again set a stiff task on reappearance at Longchamp; up against it.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at this venue (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in C&D Group 3 on reappearance in April; ran as well as could have been expected when second to the smart See The Fire in a four-runner Group 2 event at York last month.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Last season's best performances came in Group 1s, runner-up in the Irish Oaks (1m4f, good) and close fourth in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft), just behind One Look; well held by Estrange on Ascot running; this season's form is unconvincing; visored now.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Group 2 winner 6f at this venue last summer and close second to Precise in the Moyglare; well beaten in the Cheveley Park, and down the field in the Nell Gwyn on seasonal debut; tackled 1m for the first time when sixth of 11 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; plenty of stamina in the family, but hard to make a case for her.

RaceName: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: By Frankel, dam Thundering Nights won this event in 2021; Group 3-placed on second start at two; beaten favourite in a maiden on seasonal debut before comfortable win in a Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) in April; confidently ridden before taking full command in the final furlong in the Oaks; will be hard to beat if the ground is not too quick.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced sort who made a winning start for Marco Botti in 7f Wolverhampton handicap on Boxing Day; faded as if possibly needing his first outing since when sixth of ten at Sandown (7f, good) 14 days ago; entitled to come on for the run.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: No wins since a 10-1 success on his debut at Ayr a year ago but he made a bold bid from the front when second in a quite valuable handicap over 7.5f here (good to soft) a fortnight ago; same mark today and dangerous if able to get loose on the front end again.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: Has a very mixed record across his 11 starts; good second in 7f handicap at the York Dante meeting in May but has failed to reproduce it in two subsequent outings.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: Left his stable debut well behind when sixth of 22 in traditionally strong York handicap (6f, good) a fortnight ago; the way he kept on in the closing stages suggests this return to 7f may suit; considered.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: First start beyond 6f when winning Ffos Las maiden (7.3f, heavy) last September and followed up in last-gasp fashion in valuable EBF Final at York (7f, good) in October; never involved in two competitive handicaps this season but he met trouble on the first occasion and a slow start didn't help last time; will need to break better if he's to take advantage of his inside stall.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: A four-time AW winner (from 5f to 7f) this year and ran really well when third over 7.5f here (good) in May; too keen when well held in big-field Goodwood handicap (7f) but looked back in form when sixth of 12 at York (6f, good) 15 days ago; drawn widest.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: Built upon the promise of her debut fourth at Newbury (7f, good) in April when winning maiden at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month; had soft ground as excuse for a disappointing run under a penalty at Salisbury 18 days ago; retains unexposed potential now handicapping.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
Spotlight: First run since being gelded when off the mark in 6f handicap here (soft) 15 days ago; led late on in a style which suggests he should be as effective back at 7f and the Richard Spencer stable continues in excellent form.

RaceName: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap
OwnerName: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Doncaster (6f, heavy; also won on good) in November and continued in very good form when placed in four handicaps this season, including second over 7.5f here (soft) 15 days ago; should be thereabouts again.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: 1m AW maiden winner in November for Ger Lyons; encouraging stable debut when beaten just over 2l at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) in April but it all went wrong when racing far too freely over 1m1f three weeks ago; takes a sharp drop in trip.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner who reappeared having been gelded with a modest effort here in May; much better a fortnight later when fifth of 12 at Thirsk (also 5f, good); stays 6f and could step forward again.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent in first four starts, including an AW 5f win in November, but he took it up a notch when a neck second of 19 at Ascot (6f, good) seven weeks ago; raised 5lb but he's a leading contender.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: A good start to his career included a Thirsk maiden (5f, good to firm) win in August but his form tailed off (mitigating circumstances) subsequently and he was never a factor when beating one home on his return at Thirsk in April; off since.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Very promising debut when 50-1 second in C&D novice (good) in October and heavy ground was a plausible excuse when he underwhelmed next time; scored with authority on his return in Pontefract novice (6f, good to soft) nearly twelve weeks ago; not seen since but this opening mark looks fair and he's much respected.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: She was dropped to this grade when running well for second at Musselburgh (5f, good) three weeks ago; same mark and stays this trip so she ought to be on the premises.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: On the up since joining current yard, landing 7f Lingfield maiden (good) in May before he was beaten two short heads at Chester (7.5f, soft) a fortnight ago; runner-up was a convincing winner at Carlisle on Wednesday; up 2lb and he's a big player if this drop back down to 6f proves no barrier.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: 6f Redcar maiden winner whose best form came when runner up here (5f, good to firm) and at Pontefract (6f, good) in June; not beaten far on her handicap debut at Hamilton the following month but subsequently absent; look for market support on this comeback.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Sent off 5-4 when beating six rivals on his debut at Newcastle in December; that race contained subsequent winner but he was turned over at 8-15 when second on his return at Wolverhampton (again 6f, AW) this month; slightly caught napping that day and he brings plenty of potential to this turf/handicap debut.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Nicely backed 100-30 shot when going in first time at Kempton in December but he was another to suffer an odds-on reversal on his second start when third at Newcastle (also 6f, AW) the following month; subsequent absence suggests there may have been an excuse and he's an interesting contender on this turf and handicap debut; cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: He was stuck in the placings when running well this spring and again couldn't quite put it to bed when runner-up at Chester (6f, soft; acts on quicker) a fortnight ago; mark is creeping up but it would be no surprise to see him in the thick of it once more.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't really build on a good start for Richard Hannon and he took a couple of goes to warm up for this yard; much better last time, however, when second of 13 at Thirsk (6f, good) three weeks ago; now he needs to kick on from that.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice last summer; 0-8 in handicaps but he's started to knock on the door from this reduced mark with a pair of third-place finishes this month; this is a better race but newly applied cheekpieces might eke out more.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap
Spotlight: Finished runner-up on his first three starts over 5f/6f on AW but his turf form isn't comparable, including when beaten 12l in a visor (discarded) last Friday; others hold more pressing claims.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: It wasn't the strongest Nottingham (5f, good to soft) novice that he won on his debut 16 days ago but he did it easily and that despite greenness; always encouraging when one from the yard wins first time out; strong claims despite a 6lb penalty.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 20,000euros yearling, £80,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; dam placed 5f/5.7f (RPR 69), half-sister to winners Spiritual Star (useful 7f-8.6f including 2yo) and Lady Princess (6f 2yo Listed); stable's newcomers often better for a run but they have had a newcomer win on AW this season.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing, J Craggs & Partner
Spotlight: Too green to do himself justice on his debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but he was staying on well when the penny dropped and would have snatched third in a couple more strides; although that was a lowly Band D maiden, he was notably strong in the market and should be capable of better; sire's progeny have a much better record on AW than turf so cause for optimism there too; this does look warmer though.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 65,000euros yearling, 75,000euros breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Smokey Bear (including 2yo/AW/Hong Kong; RPR 102); dam French 12.5f AW winner (70), half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Shamdinan and Australian 1m4f Group 2 winner Shahwardi; by a speed influence but the bottom line of his pedigree raises slight doubts over 5f.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal of a multiple 6f winner (including the Group 3 Chipchase Stakes run on this card five years ago), herself from a good family; looks the part on paper and he's the most interesting of the newcomers.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 22,500gns yearling, £30,000 breeze-up 2yo (private sale); sixth foal; half-brother to winners Knebworth (5f-6f including AW 2yo; RPR 98) and Golden Rainbow (5f including 2yo/AW; 77); dam unraced half-sister to prolific 5f winner Archimedes, out of 6f Listed winner; stable's only 2yo winner this season came in a seller.

RaceName: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Both runs over 6f on good ground; her debut second hasn't really worked out and although it appeared a backwards step when beaten over 5l at Goodwood five weeks ago, the winner ran a fine race at Royal Ascot; this stiff 5f should be fine and she is one to take seriously.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner on the Flat on good/good to firm ground; some useful runs in defeat over hurdles; something amiss over hurdles last time; started Flat campaign with a good run over 1m4f at Leopardstown.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 6-37 on turf; best form at Galway, the scene of three of his wins; in contrast, he has a weak Curragh record, unplaced in eight visits; pleasing second at Leopardstown recently on seasonal debut; a reproduction would put him in the mix.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Won two Kempton novices at 7f/1m in 2023; close up in Grade 3 at Keeneland in April 2024; no better than fourth in eight runs (six on AW) since joining this yard; well held by Clear Quartz on Leopardstown running; hard to fancy despite having a top apprentice aboard.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
OwnerName: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin
Spotlight: Won AW maiden in January; returned to turf with good efforts in 1m2f handicaps at Leopardstown; soft ground may not have been ideal at the same venue on latest; good chance here for Sophie Carter who won this event in 2024 for Gordon Elliott.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Won over the extended version of this trip at Dundalk last November on stable debut; second over 1m at the same venue on final start last year; resumed with another 1m second at Leopardstown; not beaten far in a 20-runner contest here last time, definite chance on overall form; cheekpieces fitted.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Won over an extended 1m2f at Naas last August on good ground; not suited by heavy ground on her final two starts; in rear on soft on return; much better when fourth at Leopardstown but poor again at Gowran; stablemate Fixation appeals more.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: All four wins on AW; weak turf record (0-6) is a concern; booking of the very experienced Siobhan Rutledge is the main positive; new headgear combination.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Much better record on AW (three wins from nine starts); 1-11 on turf and needs to improve on recent efforts; very capable claimer booked.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Sandown 1m handicap winner for William Haggas (1m, good); poor in a claimer and two maiden hurdles for new yard, though mishap when carried out early at Clonmel last time; cheekpieces added to tongue-tie worn since joining this yard.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Only win from 22 starts came on heavy ground at Gowran last September; failed to retrieve her best form in her first four outings this term, including a Leopardstown race won by Diamond Exchange; placed twice at Leopardstown since then, both races run on soft; capable 7lb claimer

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: 0-16 but placed five times; good runs back-to-back when fourth and third at Leopardstown and Limerick in April (both yielding); poor run on good ground on penultimate start, more like it on an easier surface last time; stable had a winner at Naas this week.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden whose best run last season was on heavy ground; unplaced in two handicaps this term and held by Great Mover based on a clash at Leopardstown.

RaceName: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth of 21 on C&D handicap debut last summer was his best run for Natalia Lupini; no show on last month's Roscommon comeback; much better when a long-priced third at Leopardstown; relatively unexposed and could feature with 7lb off.

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Rattled off a quick hat trick last season including when winning this race by a wide margin; has joined a new yard since but she goes well fresh and is very well in at the weights here; Providing she's fit enough she's the one to beat. (R14 W4 P5) (OR 87)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Finished in front of Fareedhat El Izz at Lingfield last season but that was in handicap company, and they meet on very different terms here; shaped as though the run was needed on her first start of this season for a new yard, her prominent style of racing should be suited to the track and if the likely favourite under performs she seems the most likely to take advantage, with blinkers going on for the first time. (R9 W1 P5) (OR 69)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Maiden, who's best run came in this race last year when she finished a remote third; her two runs this season haven't been without promise and if she gets a strong pace to chase again, she's capable of hitting the frame again with cheekpieces on for the first time. (R9 W0 P2) (OR 41)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Has been a real credit to connections over the years winning twice last year after finishing fourth in this race last year; ran as well as could have been expected from a long way out of the weights at Bath last time out and holds place claims. (R16 W3 P7) (OR 56)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Has only two wins to her name but she's been largely consistent in her career and ran a fine race to be second in this race last year; bumped into an improver on her seasonal return over further when she had three of these behind. Another who likes to be ridden prominently and hold place claims again. (R19 W2 P11) (OR 55)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Has yet to add to her first win in her Juvenile season but did run well here over further last season when runner-up; as work to do on her two runs this season, but a stronger pace here might help as she stays well. (R10 W1 P6) (OR 52)

RaceName: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)
Spotlight: Half Sister to three previous winners, all who where placed in pattern company; interesting that connections pitch her in at this level and the market will tell more; visored for her debut. (R0 W0 P0) (NOR)

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won two of her nine races last year, both on the AW, the second by 3.75l when stepped up to 1m4f on final start (cheekpieces on for second time); also has decent form on turf; could go well, but better over 1m4f, lacks a recent run and on a career-high mark.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a Nottingham handicap over 1m2f on third start last season (good to firm); several good runs without winning after that; lacks a recent run and seems high enough in the ratings at present.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made all to win Wolverhampton maiden last June (8.5f) and was a close third in a Goodwood handicap for fillies next time (1m2f, good); first run on fast ground and first run of the season, but could go well.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won twice last year, the first on good to firm at Salisbury, the second over C&D in June (good); also successful over 1m4f here in May; below-par in a Group 3 at Carlisle last time, but could bounce back.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has won two of her last three races, the first at Southwell, the latest at Sandown (good); both were over 1m, but bred to be much better suited by 1m2f and further improvement likely.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a five-runner Newcastle novice last October; highly tried on first two starts this term, but beaten 7l when second in C&D handicap on soft ground last time (winner won since); cheekpieces now tried; each-way chance.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unexposed; off the mark when beating a subsequent winner in a Redcar maiden last month (1m2f, good to firm); further progress likely; chance on handicap debut.

RaceName: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has won two of her last three races, both at around 1m on good, at Newmarket and Hamilton; 3lb higher than last time; not sure the step back up to 1m2f is ideal, although fair run on previous try at the trip.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes
Spotlight: Progressive for this yard and was unbeaten in handicaps until an excusable defeat on rain-hit ground at Epsom; probably still on a fair mark and trip/ground will be ideal; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Not as good as he was in France and last two runs have been well below his best; however, on too good a mark to dismiss and inmates from this yard can return to form from nowhere.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: 0-14 since a successful debut for Joseph O'Brien; well down the field in all three runs for this yard, all at 7f; good bit to prove and he's unraced beyond a mile.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Appeared to be found wanting for stamina over C&D two runs back but that was a warm handicap and he's since gone down narrowly in a Class 3 over 1m at Redcar (good; new tongue-tie), where he wasn't stopping; races up with the pace and firmly in the reckoning.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a 1m AW maiden in Ireland and was close up in a 1m Lingfield handicap three starts ago; however, not so good on his final two runs for Jessica Harrington and sold on for 5,000gns.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Five-time winner on a handy mark but it's been a quiet start to this season and, even if back on song, looks vulnerable to stronger stayers over this far; the return of a visor might be no bad thing.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Only his third run for Jim Goldie when scoring at Hamilton (1m1f, good) and off a 2lb rise he was a closing third over 1m1f here (good) when not assisted by his track position; bang in form and should run well.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: This trip on fast ground suits and every chance with a repeat of his two runs at Ripon; took a backward step here two weeks ago but he has C&D form and could run well.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown
Spotlight: He's 8-50 on the AW and only 1-13 on turf; he was bang there over this far and off this mark at Redcar in April but he's another in here on a recovery mission after two lesser efforts.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 1m2f winner on good ground at Nottingham in April (4lb lower) but has dropped his guard the last twice and he's been contesting Class 5s, so this demands a fair bit more even if back in the groove.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Thoroughly exposed 5yo who was in fair form until here last time when never involved from off the pace; has never won off this high a mark but what does bode well is that his last three wins have come in amateur races under Henry Callan who is back aboard for the first time since his last win in April; the cheekpieces also return after going in a new hood last time.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Potentially very well handicapped but that's for a reason as last season was mostly underwhelming; off since November.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unlike him to miss the break as last time and had been running well from 1m1f to 1m4f; the concerns are that he does look high enough in the weights and this is tougher than usual.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2-6 for this yard but both wins were at 1m4f at Class 6 level; looks worth taking on, for all that his last race on the AW didn't go entirely to plan.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time turf winner (1m1f/1m2f) who arrives in good nick after two solid placed efforts at Hamilton (1m3f/1m5f); hasn't shone in two starts here but otherwise he's more solid than most.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Dual 1m2f course winner for David O'Meara in 2024; close 100-1 fourth here (again 1m2f) in March was a standout for this yard but otherwise it's been a struggle; has quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Capable of the odd howler but he opened his account in Southwell handicap (1m4f, Tapeta) in April and similar form when third in an amateur riders' race back there three weeks later; wasn't at his best in another amateur riders' race last time but he's recovered from blips before.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner at up to 1m3f but she's disappointed in both starts this year; has dipped below her last winning mark, however, so can't be confidently ruled out tackling this trip for the first time.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Fairly lightly raced 4yo who left Harry Charlton after an underwhelming effort over C&D in October; swiftly in the groove for this yard and he followed encouraging second at Bath (1m6f, good to firm) in May by landing breakthrough success at Chepstow (1m4f, soft) three weeks ago; acts on Tapeta and he's firmly considered up 5lb.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Not at the top of her game this season but she's dropped down the weights and took a step back in the right direction when fifth at Hamilton (1m5f, good) three weeks ago; creditable third of four at Hamilton on Thursday; three wins here include one at this trip and she warrants a good look.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: AW regular who scored here (1m2f) in December and was a solid second over C&D in March; blew the start but ran respectably when fourth on turf at Chester a fortnight ago and he's handicapped to be competitive again.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Pulled up lame over hurdles on his first go for this yard and he received a 62l humbling back on the Flat at Carlisle (1m1f, soft) 19 days ago; good form case to be made on previous efforts for Ed Bethell but can't be considered until he starts showing something.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Back from a 488-day absence when suffering a heavy defeat on his British/stable debut in May; second go for this yard was better but he still faded to finish last a fortnight ago; will probably come good for respected stable at some point and the market will help to decipher if it's today.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Was having just his third go on AW when taking a Southwell handicap (1m3f, Tapeta) last month and he backed it up with slightly unlucky second at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago; 1lb nudge looks no bother at all and he's near the top of the list.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: 0-5 on AW and he was never a threat over C&D in March; remote third over 2m at Musselburgh (good) three weeks ago and he now drops back in trip with added blinkers.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: 1m AW winner for Tom Clover who has been mostly hurdling for this yard; went close in January and again in Cartmel seller on penultimate start; didn't shape with much promise in two turf handicaps (1m/1m2f) in between but his Flat mark has come down quite sharply.

RaceName: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins last season included two here at 2m; never figured in this race last term but he's been in the frame all nine starts this year and was unlucky when fifth at this track in December on his last go at 1m4f; each-way prospects.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent over 5f in February; has struggled since then; drop in class after contesting a Listed race and C&D Group 2 in May; was 20lb higher at his peak; still an entire.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
OwnerName: W Jones/D W Sloan/M C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Syndicate
Spotlight: Progressed well last year over 7f, winning twice; resumed progress on return in March when defying top-weight at Dundalk; towards the rear over C&D before more like his best when fifth of 15 over 7f at Cork 13 days ago; probably lacks the pace of several of the 6f winners in this field.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner; highlight of British form when second to Big Evs in a Listed race at York in May 2024; rated 99 at his peak. gained his first turf success off 72 when making all in a 20-runner contest over a little further than this here early this month; fair fifth under a fixed penalty in a 5f Cork event four days later; has gone up 11lb in total.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Won 7f Leopardstown maiden at two; respectable effort on comeback in 3yo handicap at this venue, weakening late over 7f; won with plenty to spare over a little further than this when apprentice-ridden at Listowel; up 7lb and no claim this time, may be vulnerable.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time turf winner, including a C&D Group 3 in 2023, Listed contests and a C&D premier handicap last July; was once rated 108, so has dropped a long way; positive third at Naas in April (6f, good); no luck in running over C&D last month; well below his best over 7f at Cork last time.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
OwnerName: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate
Spotlight: Proving hard to win with (1-25); not beaten far on last two AW runs in the spring; failed to make a significant impact in a Listowel race won by Unauthorized.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: 7f winner at two and fair fifth in a 7f Listed contest at Fairyhouse; this season's form has been uninspiring; perhaps cheekpieces will help his cause; others much preferred, including stablemate Nakamura.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Was busy at two, winning nurseries at Navan and Cork over this trip; below his best this term until returning to form to take second place behind a front-running winner Midnight Dusk at Fairyhouse; took him a while to get into the clear then; leading contender.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Useful form in two runs in decent company last summer (both over 6f; good); second of 13 at this venue; not quite as good but respectable nonetheless when third at Naas; coped with easier ground when making a winning reappearance here early this month; opening mark seems fair; strong chance.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Much stronger record on AW (5-30, placed nine times) as opposed to turf (1-24, placed four times); his run at Navan over 6f on his penultimate start was one of his better efforts on grass.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 6f Dundalk maiden in December and solid runs next three starts, 5f and 6f; poor Navan run in May possibly came too soon after his previous start; showed useful form in making all to beat Nakamura at Fairyhouse; not much between the pair and Joyful Tidings who dead-heated for fourth.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly beaten in a 6f Dundalk maiden in April before winning over 5f there 13 days later; definite merit in handicap debut at Naas last month, close up until reportedly hitting a ridge over a furlong down, before rallying; went close over the minimum trip here last time; worth considering.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Finished last on his first two starts this term; gave a reassuring display when second over 5f at Cork; winner Sarahmae franked the form with an emphatic follow-up success over the same trip at Down Royal.

RaceName: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap
Spotlight: Two of her three wins have come over 5f at this track; has finished last on both starts this term; hard to fancy against that background, despite her liking for this course.

RaceName: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark in 7f classified event at Leicester and quickly followed up in similar company at Brighton (7f) six days ago; a bit more is needed here back in handicap company under a 6lb penalty but she still must enter calculations.

RaceName: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett gelding who is on the up since sent handicapping, beaten just a head when second (Buckland Belle a neck behind) over C&D 19 days ago; a player nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Advanced her form on her turf debut when gaining a first win in C&D handicap in May; backed it up with a good third of 12 over C&D 19 days ago so she's a player off an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: T A Frost & Simon Broke & Partners
Spotlight: Tasleet filly who got back on track with third of eight in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 25 days ago, despite racing freely; not out of things eased 1lb with cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to score but she posted a good second in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap and wasn't seen to best effect when sixth in 8.5f handicap there later in March; possibilities after a break.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a small-field maiden when with Ralph Beckett for whom he was competitive in handicaps at up to 1m4f; left that yard for 90,000gns; finished behind Midnight Rumble here four weeks ago on yard debut when trying 2m for the first time; that was after a lengthy break so might well be all the sharper here, and Toby Moore is a positive booking.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Much respected on his form for Alan King, for all that he was 1-22 for that yard; following a 30,000gns sale he ran okay over C&D on last month's stable debut, but no more than that.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Both wins on the AW but runner-up in five of her seven races on the grass, the last two over C&D; clearly on a high enough mark but looks sure to give her running.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has won three of his last four and doesn't win by far so might still be ahead of the game mark-wise; stays 1m6f well so this far shouldn't be an issue, nor the track as his win two weeks ago was here (1m4f).

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ex-Charlie Johnston for whom he finished third in this last year (2lb higher); joined Rod Millman for 6,000gns; tailed off on yard debut but always in the van when battling on best at Bath (1m6f, good); only 2lb higher but he's up into a Class 4 now.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has been running well, and winning, over hurdles so fitness won't be an issue on this return to Flat action and has the form to feature off this mark; versatile ground-wise and there are no stamina issues.

RaceName: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Successful handicap debut for Ed Walker off 2lb higher last September (extended 1m3f, heavy); well short of that level in three runs for his new connections; likely best watched for now.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in a novice and two maidens, most recently when beaten 24l at Salisbury (1m2f, soft) 18 days ago; starts life in handicaps at a realistic level and any market support should be heeded.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't shine in a novice and two maidens over 1m, including when 400-1 and trailing home a distant last at Windsor six weeks ago; this handicap debut is her first realistic opportunity so interesting to see how she goes in the market now upped in trip.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Irish raider who's making his second British start after finishing fourth, beaten 12l, in 1m4f Brighton classified (good to firm) on Monday; drops in trip with cheekpieces added and this won't take much winning.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW) wasn't devoid of promise in February but she hasn't built on it; can probably be excused her latest effort when she blew the start over C&D (first-time cheekpieces) last month but her profile is not convincing.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: 10lb 'wrong' when she showed her first form to finish third at Bath this month and she backed that up when third again in Ffos Las classified (also 1m2f, good) on Tuesday; this is a quick turnaround but she's the key player.

RaceName: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Had bits and pieces of form last year but this term has been a struggle and she was tailed off on AW (1m4f) 11 days ago; return to this trip might help but that's not a strong argument.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; unraced for the Crisfords; picked up cheaply last October; failed to make a significant impact on debut at Dundalk.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Placed over hurdles, two Flat runs this month, possibly one for handicaps after this.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 100-1 when finishing towards the back on debut at this venue early this month.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Has shown some ability in two outings on soft, a possible handicap prospect in due course.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Half-brother by St Mark's Basilica to several winners, including a US Grade 3 scorer, and Greatest Drama, a 7f winner also Listed-placed over 6f for this stable; dam, by Acclamation, won in Britain and the US; hooded for debut.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: E28,000 yearling; fourth foal; by Kodi Bear out of an Iffraaj mare who won in France; best watched on debut.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: E29,000 Y, E330,000 Breeze Up purchase; had a run in a barrier trial but still looked a bit raw when sixth in an 18-runner contest over C&D early in the month; beaten only a little over 2l, and has fair prospects of turning the tables on runner-up Tide Of Fortune and fourth-placed Trek Home.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Best run was when third to a pair of solid stakes-level performers in a Listed contest last year; placed in 1m maidens this year, looked awkward under pressure when odds-on at Leopardstown; not one of his better efforts last time; 6f might be inadequate, but still of some interest, with cheekpieces and a 7lb claimer.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Plenty of merit in a debut run that saw him finish eighth of 24 at this venue; went very close here on his second start; has obvious prospects of going one better in this rematch with Trek Home (fourth) and Starborn Legend (sixth).

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan
Spotlight: Fourth on both starts, over 5f at Navan and C&D; on the latter form, has much the same chance as Starborn Legend and Tide Of Fortune in theory; rider described him as running green; improvement on the cards, but the same could be said of the other pair.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Ran twice for Paddy Twomey at two; made a good start for Gillian Scott when fourth of 12 in a C&D maiden in March, running on well in the closing stages; improved from handicap debut when second to an in-form rival here last time; can win a handicap off current mark, prospects of landing a maiden are slim.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Filly by Profitable; first foal out of an unraced half-sister to an Italian 1m Listed winner; cheap yearling; not a likely contender on debut.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Positives can be taken from her debut at Down Royal, though plenty of improvement is required, since she was beaten only 6l in fourth behind a 79-rated rival; Colin Keane takes the ride.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Has to bounce back from a poor handicap run; not as appealing as stablemate Solana Beach who would have to give her 6lb in a handicap.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Fourth foal; filly by Aclaim, sister to winners Anatoli (6f AW 2yo; RPR 85), Forza Toro (7f AW 2yo; 84) and East Bank (5f turf 2yo/7f AW; 71); dam 7f 2yo winner, out of 7f Group 3 winning sister to 6f Group 2 winner Haatef; worth noting that her three full siblings all won early in their careers.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Has had plenty of time to mature since her sole start at two; one to keep an eye on for future reference, rather than a betting proposition.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver
Spotlight: Not a bad run in mid-division on debut; safely held by three of the likely key players who reached the first six in that event; tongue-tie fitted.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Towards the back in outings at Navan and this venue last autumn; needs this for a handicap mark.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Sioux Nation filly, first foal out of a 6f winner by Iffraaj; dam is a half-sister to three winners who were most effective at middle distances, out of an unraced close relation to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Walk On Bye; in good hands.

RaceName: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Cotai Glory to two winners; dam, by Tagula, an unraced sister to a 5f winner; cheap yearling; stable has had three winners in the last fortnight.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 9-1, encouraging start with fifth of nine in novice at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago; this son of Showcasing seems sure to build on it.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Son of Showcasing who has beaten one rival in a pair of 6f novices at Ascot and Nottingham this summer; needs to take a major step forward.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sergei Prokofiev colt; took a big step forward from his debut with third of nine (Nascent Star and Blondie Larry just behind) in 5f Nottingham novice 16 days ago; may do better still.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ten Sovereigns gelding; dam, unraced close relative of 1m 2yo Listed winner Classic Legend, is a half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Jallota; the market can guide for this newcomer.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 11-4 and shaped well when a debut fourth of nine in novice at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago, running on late under hand riding; this son of Starman is open to a fair bit of progress.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Encouraging debut seventh in the Brocklesby at Doncaster in March; since gelded and beat one in five-runner Goodwood maiden (5f) eight days ago; more is required.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Cheekpieces fitted and posted his best effort when sixth of seven in 5f Wolverhampton novice last month; needs another step forward here, though.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 9-1, made a highly promising start when second of nine in novice at York (5f, good) 15 days ago, running on well and just failing; this daughter of Mehmas is very much the one to beat.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Has won three races, the latest over C&D off this mark last July (good); just fair form this term, last time finishing 5l sixth to Patagonia Girl (runs in the 4.45) over C&D (good).

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Has won five on the Flat at up to 1m6f; off the mark for this Flat season when winning at Salisbury by 4.5l 13 days ago; up 6lb but a contender.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: His five wins include two here, the second over this trip on fast ground in May 2024; versatile, though, for his latest win was over 1m1f at Goodwood last month (good to soft) and last run was when fourth over 7.5f here (soft); chance back up in trip.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Has won four of his 17 turf races at up to 1m2f; respectable 2.75l third of 18 at York last time (1m2.5f good); interesting, although pulled hard and didn't seem to get home on only previous try at 1m4f.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Has won four times on the AW but is 0-23 on turf, although has been placed on 12 occasions; decent third at Bath last time (1m6f, good); each-way chance; cheekpieces now tried.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Went up 29lb for his four wins last season; wasn't at his best on first Flat run this year when last of five at Ripon (had been running well over hurdles); bit to prove.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; eight-race maiden; hasn't run to his best on the AW for Phil Kirby, but may fare better back on turf.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden; best turf run for a while when staying-on third over 8.5f at Hamilton last time (good to soft); takes a marked step up in trip.

RaceName: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo; off the mark in a Lingfield maiden last time (1m2f; soft); this step up in trip may well suit him and could go well on handicap debut.

RaceName: Truelove Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Underwent wind surgery after one run for Richard Hannon last spring; not without support on the first of his two runs back from a year off and now starts handicap life at a lowly level, stepping back up from 1m under leading apprentice Jack Callan; this task is more realistic.

RaceName: Truelove Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Wildly inconsistent this year; better from the front in a small field last time and the return to a switchback track, back up in trip, looks in his favour (fair record on the AW here); whether he reproduces the effort is the question.

RaceName: Truelove Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Often pulled hard, including in the hood, and she'll have to settle better going beyond 1m for the first time after a short break; best effort for this yard was last time, though, despite again edging left (winner progressive), and she holds claims on that.

RaceName: Truelove Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Missed the break more often than not and has still to prove his stamina over this far, having run below market expectations when last seen three months ago; questions to answer but 5lb claimer Harry Vigors has been among the winners of late.

RaceName: Truelove Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive once handicapping over this trip last year, winning four from six (three on Polytrack), latterly off a 3lb lower mark; held in two runs back but this is easier and she ought to fare better.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Benefited from the rail when winning at Ripon (6f, good) and it was always going to be tough under a penalty at Newmarket, especially back at 5f; in calmer waters here, Ashley Lewis's claim eases the extra weight and cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Initial efforts nothing to shout about, though he did show some early speed and has been gelded; may fare better.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Both runs at Chester; faced no easy task on 6f debut (good) and then ran second in a 7f seller (good to soft); showed speed that day so dropping back to 6f is no big deal; has form claims and he's now visored.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Well held in both her races, though ran on soft ground first time out (6f) and a wide draw did for her at Chester (7f, good to soft); unexposed under these conditions and might have a part to play.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive but her middle run was at Ascot (5f, good) and it wasn't a bad race at all last time at Chepstow, where she encountered soft ground for the first time; if seeing out this extra furlong she shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Weak 16-1 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and never really counted, going down by 7l; market can guide again, though.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 28-1 when a tailed-off last in a Chester 7f seller, finishing behind two of these.

RaceName: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Only ran on Tuesday when beaten just over 2l at Newbury (6f, good to firm), outperforming her odds of 100-1; one formline in that race gives her the beating of Miss Moneypit, so she's a definite contender.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: On a long losing sequence and tried in cheekpieces when beating just two in 5f handicap here five days ago; others are preferred.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: A two-time 5f/6f scorer at Southwell this winter; yet to hit top form after a three-month absence, though he was poorly drawn when 15th in 5f Epsom handicap last time; no forlorn hope in a refitted hood.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with 6f success at Lingfield in October for Harry Charlton; comes here in good nick for his new yard, finishing runner-up in 6f Newbury handicap 16 days ago; one to consider.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: Without a win since his debut in June 2024; posted a good C&D second in May but beat only one in 6f York handicap since; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: C&D scorer who got back to winning ways when making all in 5.5f handicap at Bath 22 days ago; has a career-high mark to overcome now but can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a third win of the season in 6f Salisbury handicap 18 days ago, just holding on; this reliable sort can make his presence felt again nudged up 2lb.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: A fair C&D winner at 2yrs; came in last of ten in 7f Ascot handicap on his seasonal return but this Harry Angel gelding can take a step forward now back in trip.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: Improving type who completed a 6f AW hat-trick at Lingfield this winter; just his second run on turf when last of 17 in 5f Ascot handicap last month, but poorly drawn there and not out of things.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: Upwardly mobile Showcasing gelding; made a winning start in handicaps at Bath (5.5f) in April and has advanced his form with excellent second placings at Hamilton (behind Red Spells Danger) and over C&D (beaten a nose) since; up 3lb but merits serious consideration.

RaceName: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap
Spotlight: In fine form since joining George Boughey, gaining a third AW win of 2026 at Lingfield (6f) in April; posted another good effort with third of 11 (a place behind Marcellinus) over C&D 12 days ago; must enter calculations.

RaceName: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap
Spotlight: From a speedy family; improved upon last backend's two Newmarket efforts when coming away with a long odds-on favourite to win on his Polytrack comeback here (1m) last month; taken out since on account of unsuitable (good) ground; remains open to improvement now handicapping.

RaceName: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap
Spotlight: Polytrack maiden win here last winter, when he had them nicely strung out, has been well advertised since; got upset in two runs on dirt at Meydan early in the year and has since been gelded; this task is more realistic in a hood for a yard bang among the winners.

RaceName: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap
Spotlight: Made all off 6lb lower round Musselburgh in April; has not been able to lead on either run since (fluffed the start at Beverley; said to have stopped quickly at Sandown a month ago) and has since been gelded; too soon to write him off.

RaceName: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap
Spotlight: Often pulled hard last year and was gelded over the winter; this season his issue has been blowing the start and that's what cost him any chance last time; up to winning off this mark but has to be considered risky now.

RaceName: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap
Spotlight: Said to have run flat at Chester before over-racing and not seeing out 1m4f last time; it's likely this mark is within range but she had plenty of racing last year and has again this term, and she's vulnerable to improvers now.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
Spotlight: Run to a similar level in two starts in the tongue-tie since handicapping; latest effort over 5f can be marked up a touch (stumbled at the start and came widest from the outside stall at Windsor) and he holds decent claims.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
Spotlight: Won her maiden here last summer (7f) and this is the easiest race she's run in since handicapping; two poor efforts last month will need completely overlooking however.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
OwnerName: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner
Spotlight: String of consistent efforts since her AW win last summer, including from a wide stall back on turf six weeks ago; no obvious reason she won't give her running again in a race in which a few have something to prove.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
Spotlight: Dual Wolverhampton winner earlier in the year, latterly off a 2lb higher mark, who found the race not panning out her way under a hold-up ride last time; having the vastly experience Taryn Langley take off 5lb won't hurt and she's one to consider.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
Spotlight: Wolverhampton winner for Ashley Lewis last autumn off 3lb lower; runs in a sprint for the first time since handicapping and would want to see a bit of market strength back from almost six months off.

RaceName: Bell Cleaning Handicap
Spotlight: Made all from the rail draw when winning at 33-1 off 5lb lower at Lingfield last month (first-time cheekpieces; switches to a visor tonight); since held twice at short prices and this is stronger up from 0-55 company.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Built on earlier promise this season with a comfortable victory in 5f Newmarket handicap a week ago; up 5lb but another bold showing is on the cards with Billy Loughnane now up.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Came in last on his first three starts for current yard but revitalised by cheekpieces when bagging 5f Ffos Las handicap 23 days ago; needs headgear to work the oracle for a second time off 7lb higher here.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Winless since her debut but she comes here in decent nick, not best drawn when seventh of 13 in 5f York handicap last time; not out of things off a 3lb lower mark.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 5.5f winner at 2yrs; resumed from seven months off with a good fifth of nine in 5f Goodwood handicap last month; needs considering eased 2lb.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with 5f Catterick maiden victory and signed off for Robert Cowell with a very good second of nine in 5.5f Bath handicap last month; up 3lb but very much one to consider for her new yard.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Southwell (6f) in April and has continued in good form, second of nine at Goodwood (5f) last month; ought to be thereabouts.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap
Spotlight: Keen-going sort who proved suited by dropping to 5f when gaining a first win at Lingfield in May; not best drawn when 11th in 5f York handicap last time; can make her presence felt.

RaceName: Fabric First Past The Post Handicap
Spotlight: 66-1 winner on his first Flat start since 2024 when making most to win off 2lb lower in a small field at Salisbury last month; back to his inconsistent self when tailed off at Chepstow next time and it will likely depend on what sort of mood he's in.

RaceName: Fabric First Past The Post Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Belgium/Germany (one in blinkers) who's struggled in five runs for this yard (four over hurdles); the first-time cheekpieces he sported when beaten a long way at Goodwood three weeks ago are replaced.

RaceName: Fabric First Past The Post Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-3 at Yarmouth (0-11 elsewhere, often at short prices) when beating a long odds-on shot in a three-runner race nine days ago without the usual hood; fair chance of following up off a 4lb higher mark.

RaceName: Fabric First Past The Post Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner here for Simon Dow last year who made a solid start for his new yard back on turf, having been picked up for 11,000gns, at Ripon nine days ago; just cracked late there on his first run in three months and has a good chance today.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: A fair 7f juvenile maiden but he tends to race freely and did well considering, when beaten 4l at Chester (7.5f, soft) 15 days ago, in first-time tongue-tie to go with hood (retained); takes a drop in grade and may be interesting if let loose in front.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has been placed three times on AW but not so good in two starts on turf; his mark continues to drop and first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces; needs to show more.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has shown only modest form on three runs on AW; has had wind surgery since last start in April and makes turf/handicap debut; no surprise were she to show improvement for in-form yard.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Hasn't shown much in four starts, including on handicap debut when fifth of nine at Goodwood (6f, soft) 15 days ago; steps up in trip and needs to show improvement.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 0-25 but has been runner-up five times, the last twice in classified events at Wolverhampton AW this year where his best chance of success lies; continues in new headgear combination but vulnerable to less-exposed sorts.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Unplaced in all 12 starts and she's been tailed off in three runs this year; cheekpieces back on replacing visor; others preferred.

RaceName: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has shown bits of form and was beaten less than 3l in a better contest at Salisbury (7f, good) 13 days ago; her mark continues to fall and this looks to be her best opportunity to date.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt who looked a good prospect when making a winning debut in 1m Newbury novice in October; 20-1, last of eight in Greenham Stakes there on his return; remains with plenty of potential and well worth another chance.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Not This Time gelding; fairly useful form shown when runner-up on four of his five starts, on latest back from four months off in 1m Doncaster novice 22 days ago; the form pick.

RaceName: Sporting Times Sri Lanka British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 28,000euros foal, 125,000gns yearling; half-sister to four winners, notably Stratum (1m2f-2m5f Flat, RPR 115; useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase); dam 1m winner (RPR 78); this Lope Y Fernandez filly appeals on paper but this is no easy debut; hooded.

RaceName: Choice Training Handicap
Spotlight: Three previous wins had come on the AW (5f) but he was a revelation at Newmarket eight days ago when hammering a big field over 6f; that may well have been down to the first-time eyeshield and he now has to back it up off an 8lb higher mark.

RaceName: Choice Training Handicap
Spotlight: Yet another to win first time out for this yard when turning in a career-best effort at Brighton last Sunday; makes appeal under a penalty for that, especially with Jack Callan taking off 3lb, and he looks to have Ancient State to worry about.

RaceName: Choice Training Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in good form, going close at Windsor before anticipating the start last time; losing run is 18, however, and never won off a mark this high.

RaceName: Choice Training Handicap
Spotlight: His four wins have all come over 6f on the AW and he's 0-10 on turf; having a rare start over 5f tonight and will need to improve on recent efforts.

RaceName: Choice Training Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins on the AW since finishing second in a classified at Yarmouth last summer (0-10 on turf) and he is 26lb higher than that day; would be a surprise winner back from a few months off.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced on turf; out of the frame in six starts since winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January; takes a drop in grade and as low in the weights as he's ever been; not out of this.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Promise in AW novices and on handicap debut when third at Doncaster (7f, good) last June; has since had wind surgery and should be sharper following his return from ten-month layoff when sixth of seven at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month in first-time tongue-tie (retained); another to consider.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came in an AW juvenile novice in December 2024; has ran well to be third on both starts this year at Bath and Salisbury; this also represents a class drop and he shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Has won seven of his 25 races on turf; has run well in three starts since returning last month and should run his usual solid race.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Two of her four wins have came at this track, one over today's distance; fourth in both starts this year but may be vulnerable to younger rivals.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: 1-21 but ran his best race for a while when runner-up at Pontefract (1m, good) last Sunday; claims if he can back that up.

RaceName: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark on turf at the 20th attempt when accounting for three rivals at Bath (1m, firm) last month; below par when fifth of 13 at Salisbury (1m, good) 13 days ago; win record is offputting.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: A two-time 1m4f scorer in 2025; returned from a spell in Meydan with a solid fifth of 12 in 1m3f handicap at Carlisle four weeks ago; not out of things with Billy Loughnane now up.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2025 with 1m2f handicap success at Newcastle in January, but he's failed to beat a rival on his last three runs, finishing last of 16 in 1m2f Epsom handicap 22 days ago.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: Most likeable front runner who bagged a second win of 2026 in 1m2f Ripon handicap in May; unable to lead from a poor draw when 11th at York (1m2f) 15 days ago and remains very much one to consider.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: Winless since successful in Bahrain (1m2f) in January 2025, but he got back on track with fourth of seven in 1m3f handicap at Hamilton last month; this veteran is no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
OwnerName: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks
Spotlight: Brother to the top-class Cracksman; made good strides for Dylan Cunha last season (scored twice at up to 2m) and he shaped encouragingly over an inadequate 1m2f when fourth of eight in handicap at Pontefract in April; appeals as the sort to do well in 2026 and interesting now back up in trip.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: Took this in 2025 and she made a winning return over C&D last month; up 3lb but she's a likely player once more with yard going very well.

RaceName: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap
Spotlight: A two-time 1m4f scorer at Wolverhampton in the spring; rare poor effort when last of eight in 1m4f Newmarket handicap last time; the sort to bounce back.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 75,000gns yearling who's the first foal of Shandra, who won on her 2yo debut (7f) for Dermot Weld; no surprise were she to want more of a test in due course for a trainer who does much better with 2yos on the AW than turf; market will be the best guide.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo whose dam won three Listed sprints in Ireland; in at the deep end on her 6f Epsom debut (sole newcomer) when shaping with a bit of promise, and entitled to improve for last year's winning yard.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50,000euros yearling whose debut fourth on Tapeta six weeks ago (33-1) has been boosted since by those ahead of her; got chopped off early that night before sticking on, and is one to consider.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed plenty of toe in her two runs (good/soft), both in the hood; inclined to hang left each time, though, and she'll need to improve; nurseries may be her time to shine.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful pedigree but easy to back on two runs last month and finished well held; trainer's strike-rate with juveniles is up this season but it may be in nurseries that she makes her mark.

RaceName: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 360,000gns breeze-up purchase who made a satisfactory debut behind subsequent Queen Mary (Group 2) runner-up Senorita Bonita at Nottingham (5f, good) early in the month; just about sets the standard on that and is entitled to improve.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins came over 6f in 2023 and 2024 when trained by Tim Easterby; no joy in four starts for David Loughnane and steps back to 5f on his stable debut; on a career-low mark and market watch is advised.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Has eight wins on turf but losing run is mounting up; needs to rediscover his form.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: 0-11 but finished third twice in the spring; drops back in trip and still has time on his side; should not be far away if he shows his best form.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner
Spotlight: Six of his eight wins have been at this track, the last three over today's distance; must come into calculations.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success on turf came in 2023 when trained by Craig Lidster; has win three times since on AW and left Ivan Furtado last month; unplaced in three runs for current yard and doesn't look ready to capitalise on career-low mark.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Scored at 80-1 to land a classified contest over C&D last July but ran poorly in handicaps subsequently; makes his first appearance of the year but hard to fancy.

RaceName: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Too Darn Hot filly; has been brought along steadily, a patiently ridden fourth of six in 9.5f Wolverhampton novice last month; appeals as a likely improver now going handicapping stepped up in trip.

RaceName: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Reliable Gleneagles filly who came clear of the rest when a very good second of seven in 1m4f Newbury handicap 16 days ago; big player off the same mark in her bid for a breakthrough victory.

RaceName: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Time Test filly who is a half-sister to the stable's smart Gold Cup winner Courage Mon Ami; showed promise when in the frame (up to 1m2f) at Wolverhampton and Lingfield on her first two starts but failed to build on that when a free-going fifth of six in 1m4f novice at Newcastle last month; remains with potential though now going into handicaps for top yard.

RaceName: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Nathaniel filly; in the frame on her first five starts, blinkered when a good second over C&D in May; only fifth of seven in 1m4f Newbury handicap last time but goes in different headgear here and no surprise to see her bounce back.

RaceName: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first success at the tenth attempt at Nottingham (1m2f) in May and posted a solid fourth of ten (off a 7lb higher mark) over 1m2f here 26 days ago; not out of things stepping up in trip.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Arrived following a win when a below-par fourth in this race last summer; always travelling well when winning at Chepstow 12 days ago, however, and she sneaks in here on the ceiling mark having been put up 2lb; player from a handy draw.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Trainer won with three of his last five runners coming into today, all three doing it easily, two at big prices; this triple AW winner has shown enough since returned to turf to suggest he can be a factor at this level.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Bit more hope in his two starts in the tongue-tie for his new yard; didn't appear to quite see out 1m on soft ground behind Heer's Sadie and Nicely Curved last time and he's one of the more interesting runners.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Maiden (0-9 in the US) who's been patchy for her current yard (in season when well beaten at a short price on the AW here in February before not taking to hurdles); gave hope when an unlucky-in-running third at Yarmouth last time, however; player.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Consistent last year but has lost her way since over a variety of trips; this is her first crack at 0-50 company but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Ran only once for William Haggas (poorly) and has achieved little for her current yard; first-time headgear needs to make a big difference.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Again didn't convince on the stamina front over 7f (soft) last time and she's drawn wide going up in trip again; bit to find coming out of handicap company.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in three runs over 6f; this is more realistic in a first-time tongue-tie but seeing some market interest would be wanted before she's considered.

RaceName: Animus Associates Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Huge prices for all three runs, finishing well beaten, and it's hard to find any positives from stall 1.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: 1-25 but he went close over 7f at Wolverhampton in March and his Bath third last month (5.7f, good to firm) was another sound effort; should remain competitive with first-time cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Last of five wins came at this track (7f) in May 2025 off this mark; returned to form when beaten a head here (7f, good) 12 days ago but not sure the step back to 6f for the first time since February 2024 will be in his favour.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Richard Fahey in 2024; not added to that tally since, and has been below-par in three starts for new yard; tries a new trip and runs with first-time tongue-tie and blinkers which need to have a positive impact.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Back to form when scoring over C&D (good to soft) last month and has made the frame twice since over C&D; she looks sure to be thereabouts.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins came at Southwell AW last year; down the field twice last month back on turf and best to leave until returning to Southwell.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Still a maiden and lacks consistency to boot; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Reecer Group Handicap
Spotlight: Has struggled since landing two wins in the summer of 2024; another change of headgear with first-time blinkers taking over from a visor.

RaceName: Harris & Bailey Builders Merchants 'Confined' Apprentice Handicap (Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Well suited by undulating tracks and has come back to form since undergoing wind surgery; now 3lb below her last winning mark and Harry Vigors has plenty of experience for 5lb in this company;  down in class and it's hard to find any negatives.

RaceName: Harris & Bailey Builders Merchants 'Confined' Apprentice Handicap (Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Arron D'Arcy (1-10 overall) rode a winner for the trainer, who won this race two years ago, early in the month; no problem with 7f under him last time but the drop back in trip, minus the usual hood, should be fine; player.

RaceName: Harris & Bailey Builders Merchants 'Confined' Apprentice Handicap (Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Excuses since returned to turf, and having Jack Callan up is an obvious plus, but she's an exposed maiden whose best efforts have come on AW; the cheekpieces experiment has quickly been binned and she needs to find more from somewhere.

RaceName: Harris & Bailey Builders Merchants 'Confined' Apprentice Handicap (Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Perhaps the return to turf will prove the answer but he hasn't shone in two runs in cheekpieces this year, having been gelded, and is regressive; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Gran Premio Estrellas Mile - Auspicia Laboratorio Cimol Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)

Stephen Foster Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
Spotlight: Game front-runner; successful on last two starts in Grade 3 Kentucky Cup Classic (1m1f, Tapeta) and black type event at Oaklawn Park (8.5f, dirt); will ensure a good gallop but has a mountain to climb in this grade.

RaceName: Stephen Foster Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: C2 Racing Stable LLC, Gary Barber & La Milagrosa Stable LLC
Spotlight: High-class form at best, winning four Grade 1s including 2023 Breeders' Cup Classic at Santa Anita and 2025 Pegasus World Cup (1m1f) at Gulfstream Park; returned to form when winning Grade 2 Oaklawn Handicap (1m1f) last time by 2l from Sovereignty, stalking behind pace duel and powering past tiring leaders; should give another good account.

RaceName: Stephen Foster Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
Spotlight: US Horse of the Year in 2025; won Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs and three times at Saratoga afterwards, notably Grade 1 Belmont Stakes and Grade 1 Travers (by 10l); shaped as if needed race when 2l second to White Abarrio in Grade 2 at Oaklawn on reappearance, duelling hard with Journalism while travelling strongly, fading in straight; sure to have improved considerably for the run and will take a lot of beating, particularly as he's won both his starts here.

RaceName: Stephen Foster Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
OwnerName: C R K Stable LLC & Grandview Equine
Spotlight: Smart horse on his day who beat Magnitude by just over 2l in Grade 1 Pennsylvania Derby (1m1f) last September; creditable, three-quarter-length third to Corporate Power in Grade 2 Alysheba at Churchill Downs on reappearance, again slowly away; capable of big run with smooth getaway (has undergone remedial work) and not the worst outsider.

RaceName: Stephen Foster Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)
Spotlight: Has improved into high-class colt as 4yo, winning Grade 3 Razorback Handicap at Oaklawn (8.5f; by 4l) in February and Grade 1 Dubai World Cup at Meydan (1m2f) in March; made all and found extra to hold Forever Young by 1l in the latter, showing really deep stamina and great courage; faces pressured pace this time and may not be at his sharpest after three-month layoff but still deserves so much respect.
